issue_title: max_worker_per_child doesn't work on Windows (with a fix)
issue_body:
Parameter `max_memory_per_child` is not passed to `Worker.__reduce__` method:

https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/471d38e72df9cf380be90f89d0dde2735073b851/billiard/pool.py#L267-L272

As a result, on Windows, where forking is made via multi-processing, a child process doesn't receive such parameter. So, an automatic restart of a worker after reaching a memory limit doesn't happen.

To fix this issue, the method `Worker.__reduce__()` should be modified to:

```
    def __reduce__(self):
        return self.__class__, (
            self.inq, self.outq, self.synq, self.initializer,
            self.initargs, self.maxtasks, self._shutdown, self.on_exit,
            self.sigprotection, self.wrap_exception, self.max_memory_per_child
        )
```

issue_title: s3 paginator is encoding the prefix, which means that paginators are broken in this context
issue_body:
I am using Amazon cognito so my users can save files directly into an S3 bucket.

As a result, the files are saved with a ket that looks like this:

us-east-1:1e3ee2dd-4537-4aa2-95d4-fadfd6828388/blah/myfile.txt

This bit:
"us-east-1:1e3ee2dd-4537-4aa2-95d4-fadfd6828388"

Is the users Cognito ID and is created by Amazon and is thus out of my control.

And my goal is to process all the file that belong to that user.

So the best way to do that is use a paginator with a prefix set to "us-east-1:1e3ee2dd-4537-4aa2-95d4-fadfd6828388"

BUT boto3 appears to be url encoding the prefix and thus no results are returned by the paginator.

Could you please turn off the url encoding of the prefix on paginators?

thanks

issue_title: Allow disabling S3 ETag checking
issue_body:
Some S3 implementations, e.g. [S3Proxy](https://github.com/andrewgaul/s3proxy) using the Azure or GCS storage backends, do not have MD5 ETags.  Thus methods like `Key.set_contents_from_filename` spuriously fail with:

```
boto.exception.S3DataError: BotoClientError: ETag from S3 did not match computed MD5
```

Could boto add a configuration to disable this check?  This would be similar to aws/aws-sdk-java#560.

issue_title: Relax upper bound on python-dateutil
issue_body:
PR #1402 was over-zealous in tightening the upper bound on `python-dateutil`. As you can see in that thread and in #1406, this is causing problems for a lot of people, apparently.

As [my comment on 1406 indicates](https://github.com/boto/botocore/pull/1406#issuecomment-377535983), I think that it *should* be fine to just revert #1402, that said, the "belt and suspenders" approach for people stuck with a version of `pip` that does not support `Requires-Python` would be to add conditional requirements in [`requires`]:

```python
requires = ['jmespath>=0.7.1,<1.0.0',
            "python-dateutil>=2.1,<2.7.0; python_version == '2.6'",
            "python-dateutil>=2.1,<3.0.0; python_version >= '2.7'",
            'docutils>=0.10']
```

While you're at it, I think using an `if` statement on `sys.version_info` in `setup.py` is *also* the wrong thing to do here, because the version that executes `setup.py` is often but not always the same version that the package is being installed for:

```python
requires = ['jmespath>=0.7.1,<1.0.0',
            'docutils>=0.10',
            # python-dateutil dropped support for Python 2.6 in version 2.7.0
            'python-dateutil>=2.1,<2.7.0; python_version == "2.6"',
            'python-dateutil>=2.1,<3.0.0; python_version >= "2.7"',
            # For python2.6 we have a few other dependencies.
            # First we need an ordered dictionary so we use the
            # 2.6 backport.
            'ordereddict==1.1; python_version == "2.6"',
            # Then we need simplejson.  This is because we need
            # a json version that allows us to specify we want to
            # use an ordereddict instead of a normal dict for the
            # JSON objects.  The 2.7 json module has this.  For 2.6
            # we need simplejson.
            'simplejson==3.3.0; python_version== "2.6"'
            ]
```

issue_title: What would be the implications of disabling validation in serialization?
issue_body:
In an application that uses boto to massively insert to DynamoDB we've been able to observe how a significant amount of CPU time is dedicated to serialization and a part of it is dedicated parameter validation. 

Disabling validation (through forcing ``include_validation=False`` in ``create_serializer()`` at serialize.py) leads to a ~23% performance increase. 

Now, the question is: **how (un)safe would be to disable validation in a production system?**

python 2.7 
Amazon Linux AMI 2015.09
botocore 1.3.15

issue_title: Firefox 41 workaround: Unable to connect to host 127.0.0.1 on port 7055 after 45000 ms
issue_body:
Firefox 41 allows only installation of signed extensions. Selenium does not have a signed extension available yet. Running Splinter on Firefox 41 fails with `Unable to connect to host 127.0.0.1 on port 7055 after 45000 ms`

You can make Firefox to work again by dropping:

       firefox_profile.set_preference('xpinstall.signatures.required', False)

to  `firefox.py`.

This workaround won't work for Firefox 42.

More information

* https://github.com/SeleniumHQ/selenium/issues/927

* https://github.com/SeleniumHQ/selenium/issues/901

Just for your information. I guess this issue can be closed when Selenium gets a proper release.

issue_title: Add support for TAB or ENTER keys in browser.type
issue_body:
Dear splinter team,
I'm using `django-froala-editor` in my projects and want to run behavior tests. As it is a bit tricky to get the `FroalaField` inputs as this are contenteditable DIVs. I'm using the following approach with navigating with TAB keys:
```
import splinter
from selenium.webdriver.common.keys import Keys
from selenium.webdriver.common.action_chains import ActionChains

with browser as b:
    b.visit("/")
    # click on an visible element
    self.browser.find_by_id("id_foo").first.click()
    # next element is a FroalaFieldInput and is defined by tab order
    actions = ActionChains(b.driver)
    actions.send_keys(Keys.TAB)
    actions.send_keys("AB")
    actions.perform()
```

I would like to somehow have a more "natural" possibility to type "TAB" or other special keystrokes, e.g. by passing selenium `Keys` to `browser.type()`. I think as this is implemented in Selenium it could be useful in splinter, too.

issue_title: django 1.9.x compatibility
issue_body:
with django 1.9.2 I run into an ImportError:

> File "/[path-to-site-packages]/django_libs/format_utils.py", line 19, in <module>
>     from django.utils.importlib import import_module
> ImportError: No module named importlib


In the given django version there is no importlib module anymore.
A small change like the following did the trick (for me):

```
try:
    from importlib import import_module
except ImportError:
    from django.utils.importlib import import_module

```

issue_title: S2L and Selenium 3 protocol?
issue_body:
We have a large existing codebase using Appium 0.18.2 along with Robot Framework and the Selenium2Library. The testing centers around an Android wrapped application, and works great with the Appium 0.18.2 server.

This breaks, however, when upgrading to Appium 1.x.  In this case, the switch to webview fails because doing so now requires the use of driver.contexts, which is not currently available with S2L.  The 'migrating to 1.0' page on the appium site explains it better than I can:

(See 'Hybrid support through context, not window')
https://github.com/appium/appium/blob/master/docs/en/migrating-to-1-0.md

I raised an issue with the Appium dev and he suggested the following:

"I think Robot Framework probably needs to be updated so that it can work with the selenium 3 protocol. Maybe someone can make an Appium extension to Robot Framework.

The only workaround I can suggest right now is patching the driver object you get with Robot Framework to include the context commands. You can take a look at the appium-client code to see how that's done."

That's way beyond my current coding capabilities, unfortunately.  Is support for the Selenium 3 protocol something you might be addressing yourself in the future?

issue_title: Capture last install/synced Pipfile.lock hash in virtualenv to skip redundant installs/syncs
issue_body:
I'm always frustrated when install/syncs take significant amounts of time when the lockfile hasn't changed. It'd be handy to have this handled automatically.

We can capture last installed/synced Pipfile.lock hash (meta/hash or similar) in virtualenv directory to skip redundant installs/syncs.

Command line parameters, redirecting to other files, and makefile/other infra changes are more time intensive workarounds that require pipenv introspection. Capturing hashes per venv would be a handy universal solution.

issue_title: Question: dev/deploy workflow
issue_body:
Hi. I'm looking for some guide/tutorial on using pipenv in development-delivery cycle, currently I'm missing two key points in docs:
1. What is proper way to deploy microservice based on Pipfile[.lock] (i.e., how to build docker container?).
My feeling is that during deployment only .lock should be read, as described set of dependencies developed/tested with, but it's not clear what's the proper way:
    - `sync` seems to be appropriate (reads .lock file, installs packages), but doesn't support `--system` (addressed in #2227) ­— which is default approach while building containers
    - `install --system` is ok for installing in container, also support `--deploy` — is it defferent from `sync`? Docs says "This will fail a build if the Pipfile.lock is out–of–date" — does it check against PyPI or Pipfile?
    - `install --ignore-pipfile` — is it the same as `sync`?
2. What's the way to use editable installs while developing? Pretty often I develop library and app (using that library) at the same time — to ensure compatibility/ease of interface use/etc.
While working with pip I can do one-timer like `pip install -e path/to/local/lib`, or manage some `requirements-dev` that's local for every developer. During dev install we can feed all requirement files, for CI — only libraries from upstream.
I'm looking for something similar with `pipenv` — support for `[dev-packages]` is perfect in terms of handling priorities during install and locking, but I can't put there any "per developer"-specific configuration. And afaik, `pipenv` can not work with multiple Pipfiles.

Originally reported by: **ikelos (Bitbucket: [ikelos](http://bitbucket.org/ikelos), GitHub: [ikelos](http://github.com/ikelos))**

----------------------------------------

issue_title: question about "install_requires"  PEP 508  support in a setup.py
issue_body:
Hi,

I would like to know if, in a setup.py, used to prepare a wheel, the PEP 508 applies fully.

Case at hand:

issue_title: setuptools wheel install will fail if a data dir is a subdirectory in a package
issue_body:
With the new wheel support, I ran into an issue where if `data_files` specifies a subdirectory in a package, `setuptools` will fail on moving the data.  This worked in previous versions of setuptools w/o wheel support, and also works with pip install.

Perhaps this is not a supported use case (should `package_data` be used instead?)

I was able to work around the issue by removing the subdirectory, but in case someone else runs into this, here is a repro test case that can be added to `test_wheel.py`


```
    dict(
        id='data_in_package',
        file_defs={
            'foo': {
                '__init__.py': '',
                'data_dir': {
                    'data.txt': DALS(
                        '''
                        Some data...
                        '''
                    ),
                }
            }
        },
        setup_kwargs=dict(
            packages=['foo'],
            data_files=[('foo/data_dir', ['foo/data_dir/data.txt'])],
        ),
        install_tree=DALS(
            '''
            foo-1.0-py{py_version}.egg/
            |-- EGG-INFO/
            |  |-- DESCRIPTION.rst
            |  |-- PKG-INFO
            |  |-- RECORD
            |  |-- WHEEL
            |  |-- metadata.json
            |  |-- top_level.txt
            |-- foo/
            |  |-- __init__.py
            |  |-- data_dir/
            |     |-- data.txt
            '''
        ),
    ),
```

In the test case, `foo` is a package and `/foo/data_dir/` is a data dir. Since `foo` already exists as a package, the `os.rename` fails, even though `/foo/data_dir/` does not exist. Should it check if the directory already exists?

The error looks something like the following (on OSX):

```
                for entry in os.listdir(subdir):
                    os.rename(os.path.join(subdir, entry),
>                             os.path.join(destination_eggdir, entry))
E                   OSError: [Errno 66] Directory not empty: '/var/folders/07/c66dj8613n37ssnxsvyz8dcm0000gn/T/tmpybp6f4ft/foo-1.0-py3.5.egg/foo-1.0.data/data/foo' -> '/var/folders/07/c66dj8613n37ssnxsvyz8dcm0000gn/T/tmpybp6f4ft/foo-1.0-py3.5.egg/foo'
```

issue_title: Prefixing `db_table` breaks models with long names.
issue_body:
Django will not allow a default `db_table` longer than the backend/connection allows. Fluent is explicitly assigning a `db_table` (to give it a prefix), which is disabling this truncation by Django.

I think Django should probably still truncate it to the max length allowed by the connection, even if it is explicitly set. Presumably there is no way for a table to exist in the database with a name that is longer than the max length.

However, it would take a while to get that change into Django and it probably wouldn't be backported to 1.7, 1.8, 1.9.

So, `django-fluent-contents` and `django-fluent-pages` should both do what Django does, and truncate the prefixed table name. Here are the offending lines:

https://github.com/edoburu/django-fluent-contents/blob/44154533c347b2985c28627cb15e4cdf7c441e76/fluent_contents/models/db.py#L177

and:

https://github.com/edoburu/django-fluent-pages/blob/master/fluent_pages/models/db.py#L55

And here's the Django code:

https://github.com/django/django/blob/7bb373e3097fe8e000e0bba005ff2dcfc18ab9a5/django/db/models/options.py#L214-L217

That Django code has been around for a long time, so it should work for all supported versions of Django, and probably several older versions too.

issue_title: balancedconsumer will get a PartitionOwnedError
issue_body:
1. start a kafka server and not include any data, create a topic with 3(any number greater than one)partition.
2. create a balancedconsumer, set `consumer_timeout_ms = -1`.
3. start the balancedconsumer client, and then start it again, here are two client named clientA, clientB.
4. after rebalance_max_retries*rebalance_backoff_ms ms, clientB will get a `PartitionOwnedError`.
5. i have debug the code, find the reason is `consume()` function will always acquire the `_rebalancing_lock` and not to release it, so `_rebalance()` will block at `with self._rebalancing_lock`.

it occur in both master and dev version.

issue_title: Improve ellipse regions
issue_body:
I'd like to propose a few changes to the ellipse regions, [EllipsePixelRegion](http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.EllipsePixelRegion.html) and [EllipseSkyRegion](http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.EllipseSkyRegion.html), that I don't have time for now to implement in #85.

- [ ] Rename parameter `major` -> `a` and `minor` -> `b`. These are the names that were previously chosen for [astropy.modeling.functional_models.Ellipse2D](http://docs.astropy.org/en/latest/api/astropy.modeling.functional_models.Ellipse2D.html) and [photutils.aperture.EllipticalAperture](http://photutils.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/photutils.aperture.EllipticalAperture.html).
- [ ] The ellipse mask test in `regions/shapes/tests/test_masks.py` currently uses `EllipsePixelRegion(PixCoord(3.981987, 4.131378), major=2.2233, minor=3.3411, angle=32 * u.deg)`, i.e. minor > major, probably due to a misunderstanding in the argument order. I suggest we change this test to use `a > b` and update the reference results files, even if `a` and `b` are no longer called major and minor.
- [ ] The description of `a` and `b` should not claim they are the major and minor axis. That's a can of worms, because sometimes `a > b` and `b < a`, we don't have any control over that in the code. This should also be fixed in the [astropy.modeling.functional_models.Ellipse2D](http://docs.astropy.org/en/latest/api/astropy.modeling.functional_models.Ellipse2D.html) docstring, I think there's already an issue with no action discussing that. We can expose extra attributes `major` and `minor` if people want, or a method that returns a new shape where always `a>b` if people think that's useful.

Just overall, I think it's important that we finish the photutils aperture -> astropy regions code transition soon and systematically and someone spend a week to do that. We're wasting time looking at the same issues that have already been discussed, sorted out and are coded up and tested in `photutils`. (I'm not saying we have to do everything the same, this is a chance to fix inconsistencies and mistakes we made in photutils if any.)

Putting this on the 0.3 milestone. Help wanted!

issue_title: Deprecate or remove requires_2to3 support
issue_body:
This is probably a discussion for the in-person coordination meeting, but I think we should consider removing support for ``requires_2to3`` (which allows 2to3 to be disabled on a per-subpackage basis) once we make sure that no affiliated packages are using it. If we make this decision, then I think the following things can be removed:

* The custom ``build`` command (``AstropyBuildPy``), because the main purpose of this command is to deal with the 2to3 option and also to separate build directories for different Python versions even for pure-Python packages, which was only needed because the Python 2 and 3 source code could be different.

* The custom ``install`` and ``install_lib`` commands (``AstropyInstall`` and ``AstropyInstallLib``) - these were only needed because we were customizing the build location with ``AstropyBuildPy``, but should no longer be needed once we use standard build directories.

* Some code in ``setup_helpers.py`` related to this option.

Overall, the main benefit would be to go back to not providing custom build, install, and install_lib commands. In general I think we should always be striving to use the standard mechanisms for package maintenance whenever possible.

Maybe we could set a deadline of 3.0 for this, and warn affiliated package maintainers so that they adjust their code base to be 2 and 3-compatible? (which would be a good thing anyway). Note that if they don't want to do that yet, they can simply avoid updating to a more recent astropy-helpers.

issue_title: Build plugin to allow example gallery to be based on docs content
issue_body:
This idea was discussed at the coordination meeting, and I did some experimental work at https://github.com/eteq/sphinx-example-docs-testing , but I don't think we wrote it down in an issue somewhere...

The idea here is to replace the example gallery currently in the astropy docs with a plugin that finds marked examples from the docs and builds a list of examples from them.  An additional stretch goal is to then *also* generate a gallery from any plots/images inside the examples.  So this issue is about making that plugin (which is necessary before the content can be moved from the existing example gallery).

Two questions that need answering:

* What's the best way to indicate "this is an example"?  The prototype work suggests the best thing is to just use a standard sphinx link, but with a standard prefix (i.e. ``.. _example-my-reference-label:``).  That's relatively easy to implement vs a dedicated sphinx directive or something like that.
* For @astrofrog : it *may* be we want to move this issue to one of the separate sphinx-related repos? Not sure which is the right place for it, though... Any thoughts? Or does it deserve its *own* separate repo?

issue_title: Better Access to Response Headers
issue_body:
On a successful API request the Stripe Java SDK doesn't provide a way of accessing response headers. (At least not that I can tell).

This can be useful in the case you're interested in response metadata (such as Idempotency-Key and Request-ID).

For example in a GET /charges/:id request:

```python
import stripe
stripe.api_key = "sk_test_..."

# list charges
stripe.Charge.list()

# retrieve single charge - no response headers
charge = stripe.Charge.retrieve("ch_1A2PUG2eZvKYlo2C4Rej1B9d")
```

It would be great if there was an optional way to grab the HTTP response metadata through some kind of bound method on the deserialized response:

```python
charge.getResponse()
```

Or simply as a property:

```python
charge.response
```

Because [api_requestor](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/api_requestor.py#L352) returns only the deserialized version of the response body it isn't possible to get HTTP request-specific things at the moment. Does it make sense to add a `response` property here so that we mirror the approach [discussed in the node sdk](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/issues/406)?

issue_title: Greycoprops: Energy Computation Error
issue_body:
## Problem
The feature named 'Energy' which can be obtained from the `greycoprops(P, prop='feature'')` method is computed with the wrong equation ([greycopops documentation](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.feature.html#skimage.feature.greycoprops))

The Energy of a signal  is defined as the sum (or integral) of the absolute value of the signal squared:
_**E=** sum (|x(n)|²)_ ([energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Energy_(signal_processing)))

Because of this, energy is exactly equal to the Angular Second Moment ([ASM](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.feature.html#skimage.feature.greycoprops)) and **not** the square root as it is curretly being computed.

Therefore, the square rooting of the ASM to compute Energy should be eliminated, since _energy = ASM_. See the[ original paper ](http://haralick.org/journals/TexturalFeatures.pdf) from Haralick

issue_title: Remove umfpack dependency from random walker segmentation.
issue_body:
This is a low priority issue as it doesn't affect random walker functionality, but as of scipy 0.14 umfpack is deprecated (see http://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy-dev/reference/release.0.14.0.html#scipy-sparse-linalg).

It's used to speed up some cases in random_walker_segmentation.py from skimage.segmentation.
There seems to be an unmaintained scikit (https://github.com/rc/scikit-umfpack). 

I think removing it from random_walker_segmentation.py is a good idea.

issue_title: Feature request: balloon segmentation
issue_body:
## Description
Hi, I found an active contour model segmentation algorithm in scikit-image and while looking up some information I came across this paper:

Cohen, L. D. (1991). On active contour models and balloons. CVGIP: Image Understanding, 53(2), 211–218. http://doi.org/10.1016/1049-9660(91)90028-n

Which seems to use a better algorithm. I've seen people use balloon segmentation and claim that it works better.

Federici, F., Dupuy, L., Laplaze, L., Heisler, M., & Haseloff, J. (2012). Integrated genetic and computation methods for in planta cytometry. Nature Methods, 9(5), 483–485. http://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1940

I hope this makes sense...

issue_title: Implement decrement for predicted segments corresponding to inactive cells
issue_body:
This task is to implement a decay that penalizes segments that were incorrectly created and cause extra predictions. If a cell is predicted, but does not become active the corresponding segment should be decremented very slightly. In the current implementation and in the whitepaper such segments are not penalized. In some situations the old behavior can cause an increasing number of extra predictions.

The algorithm functionality has been tested and verified in nupic.research. See [orphan_temporal_memory](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.research/blob/master/sensorimotor/sensorimotor/orphan_temporal_memory.py) and [corresponding simulations](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.research/tree/master/sequence_learning).

This task is to move this functionality over to NuPIC. Specifically we need to add the decrement to TemporalMemory, FastTemporalMemory and add corresponding unit tests. Setting the decrement to zero retains the old behavior.

issue_title: Conditionally add processor architecture-specific compiler/linker options
issue_body:
Developers trying to build nupic.core for ARM/Raspberry Pi encountered build errors concerning compiler/linker options that are intended only for the Intel architecture and are incompatible with ARM; reference issue #955.

```
gcc: error: unrecognized argument in option '-mtune=generic'
gcc: error: unrecognized command line option '-m32'
```

nupic.core's cmake scripts (primarily https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/CommonCompilerConfig.cmake) should be modified to add Intel-specific compiler/linker options only when targeting the Intel-compatible processor architecture.

issue_title: Obtain list of shared records on collection
issue_body:
On protected resources, where permissions are enabled, it should be possible for user to list the records on which they have a permission.

# Use cases:

* Bob wants to share some records publicly on his collection. He adds ``system.Everyone`` to the ``read`` permission on some records. Anonymous users can now obtain a list of Bob public records.
* Alice creates records on a collection, where she has no other permission than ``records:create``. She becomes the owner of those records. She must able to list them and delete them.
* Bob creates some records in his collection, on behalf of Alice. He sets the ``read`` permission to Alice on those records. Alice can now see the records that Bob created for her.

# Challenges:

* Couple storage (filtering) and permission backends in an elegant way
* Do not reject guests in authorization policy for list operation on collections

# Several strategies:

**1 Query permission backend to obtain list of ids**
In the resource code, if the user has no perm on the collection itself, add an implicit filter when querying records on storage backend. The filter is a (id, IN, <list of allowed record ids>), and the list of allowed record ids is obtained from the permission backend.

Pros:
* easiest to implement
* good separation of concern

Cons:
* does not scale (``WHERE id IN <millionrecords>``)

**2 Add a permission field in the record data**
Mix data and permissions in the JSON field of storage.
When retrieving the record data from storage, remove the permissions.

Pros:
* seemly easy to implement

Cons:
* no separation of concerns, both backends manage permission notions
* must create DB indices on JSON attributes that contain list values (e.g. ``data->permission->read``)
* transactions management between storage and permission backends ?

**3 Add permissions notion in storage**
Add new table in storage backend, index and query those columns.
Pros:
* efficient (i.e. scales)

Cons:
* no separation of concerns, both backends manage permission notions
* transactions management between storage and permission backends ?

**4 Move permissions to storage**
Leave in permissions the user principals operations.
Manage permissions from storage. Each record has a set of permissions, and the authorization queries the storage backend to know who is allowed.

Pros:
* Clean separation of concern
* Permissions are linked to record using foreign keys, and we get rid of 'object URIs' to refer to objects
* Efficient (scales)

Cons:
* a lot of code to rewrite

- [x] Check and merge PRs if possible

Release!

- [x] Upload to pypi
- [x] Mailing list announcement
- [x] Blog post

issue_body:
A few months ago, we had a crisis over historical price from Yahoo. It was fixed in `0.5.0` release. Now `get_quote_yahoo` is the new victim:

```
In [1] web.get_quote_yahoo('DVAX')
RemoteDataError: Unable to read URL: http://finance.yahoo.com/d/quotes.csv?s=DVAX&f=sl1p2rt1s7

In [2] web.get_quote_google('DVAX')

I understand the `get_quote_yahoo` function is experimental. But It would be nice if we can fix it.

issue_title: UnicodeDecodeError on scraping data from multiple sources
issue_body:
When I run the following code I get `UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte [x] in position [y]: invalid continuation byte`, where `[x]` & `[y]` vary depending on the requested stock data or source:
```
from pandas_datareader import data, wb
import datetime

start = datetime.datetime(2017, 1, 1)
end = datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 1)

bac = data.DataReader('BAC', 'google', start, end)
```

I've tested on various sources (3 so far on 4 or 5 stocks) and I consistently get this error.
I'm running this on a conda environment, where the python's version is `3.6` and `pandas_datareader`'s version is `0.5.0`.

Could someone point out what is the issue here?

issue_title: Permission to generate stubs for python-fire in typeshed
issue_body:
Hi Folks,

I would like to add stubs for _python-fire_ to https://github.com/python/typeshed, a repository used to store PEP 484 type signatures for the Python stdlib and popular third party libraries. As per PEP 484, permission is required from the library owner(s) before merging type signatures into typeshed - https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/#the-typeshed-repo

These stubs will be used by mypy and Pycharm. I'll start working on creating stubs after I get permission from the owners or maintainers of _python-fire_.

Thank You.

issue_title: contrib.flask: request handler exceptions are reported twice
issue_body:
I'm seeing two sentry messages sent for each exception:

- one as a result of `got_request_exception` signal sent from [here](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/flask/blob/1d49343bb19933f5f8057a548cb69657d8cbd699/flask/app.py#L1537)

- the other as a result of error being logged from [here](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/flask/blob/1d49343bb19933f5f8057a548cb69657d8cbd699/flask/app.py#L1550)

I wouldn't like to set `register_signal=False`, because I'd lose `X-Sentry-Id` header. I wouldn't like to set `logging=False`, because I'd lose capturing of non-exception errors. For now I'm working this around by setting `app.config['RAVEN_IGNORE_EXCEPTIONS'] = [Exception]`.

Is this the expected behaviour? If it is, is there a better way to avoid dupes?

issue_title: Raven client doesn't re-establish the connection to the server
issue_body:
We followed the official document (https://docs.sentry.io/clients/python/usage/) to use Raven client to log events to Sentry server as follows
-----------------------------------
from raven import Client
client = Client(DSN)
client.captureMessage(message)
-----------------------------------

Everything worked perfectly as long as the connection to the Sentry server was healthy.  Hoever, whenever the connection to the server was down, we had to restart our application to re-initialize the Raven client.  It seem that once the connection is down, the Raven client will stop logging all future events, even if the connection comes back later.  

The error message we got right after losing the connection was No handlers could be found for logger "sentry.errors".

It would be much better if the client could re-connect to the server when the server comes back.

Did I miss something here?

Thank you!

issue_title: raven.contrib.celery.register_logger_signal ignores subclasses of SentryHandler
issue_body:
`raven.contrib.celery.register_logger_signal` attempts to find an existing handler to add a CeleryFilter to.  It only considers existing handlers that are of type: `raven.handlers.logging.SentryHandler`.  This means that if a subclass of this handler is found (such as `raven.contrib.django.handlers.SentryHandler`) the existing handler is ignored and a brand new handler with default settings is added, resulting in two potentially duplicate handlers.

The fix for this would be to change the `type` check [here](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/raven/contrib/celery/__init__.py#L46) to an `isinstance` check.

I'm interested in submitting a PR for this with tests, if this change is considered appropriate.

Here is the skeleton of a test that would help cover this:
```
def test_subclassed_handler_updated(self):
    client = InMemoryClient()
    register_logger_signal(client)

    class CustomHandler(SentryHandler):
        pass

    root = logging.getLogger()
    handler = CustomHandler(client)
    root.addHandler(handler)

    celery.signals.after_setup_logger.send(
        sender=None, logger=root,
        loglevel=logging.WARNING, logfile=None,
        format=u'', colorize=False,
    )

    assert len(root.handlers) == 1

    handler = root.handlers[0]
    assert isinstance(handler, CustomHandler)

    assert len(handler.filters) == 1

    _filter = handler.filters[0]
    assert isinstance(_filter, CeleryFilter)
```

issue_title: date serialization failure
issue_body:
The following code:
```python
import rdflib
g = rdflib.Graph()
y = ' <http://a.example/X> <http://a.example/p> "2016-01-01T00:00:00"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime>,' \
    '                                           "2016-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime> .'

p=g.parse(data=y, format="turtle")
print(p.serialize(format="turtle").decode('utf-8'))
```
Causes an error:
```
  File "...lib/python3.5/site-packages/rdflib/term.py", line 822, in __gt__
    return self.value > other.value
TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
```

This is because the serializer is trying to sort the output tuples and the two different forms of date/time can't be sorted.

Recommend the following change - line 822, term.py
```python
            if self.value != None and other.value != None:
                try:
                    return self.value > other.value
                except TypeError:
                    return str(self.value) > str(other.value)
```
(The idea being that, if the native comparison doesn't work. fall back on the string comparison

issue_title: Rename await method of CountDownLatch
issue_body:
In Python 3.5, await and async added to the language as identifiers and in Python 3.7 they become reserved keywords. 

In CountDownLatch class, there is a method named await which causes a SyntaxError in Python 3.7. This method should be renamed to support Python 3.7.

issue_title: GalSim 1.5 is incompatible with the LSST Data Management stack
issue_body:
A few weeks ago, I tried to naively update the version of GalSim being shipped with the LSST stack.  This resulted in one of our GalSim-dependent packages breaking with the following errors
```
include/lsst/meas/extensions/shapeHSM/HsmAdapter.h:40:69: error: no matching function for call to 'galsim::ImageView<float>::ImageView(float*&, const boost::shared_ptr<float>&, const int&, const galsim::Bounds<int>&)'
```
```
include/lsst/meas/extensions/shapeHSM/HsmAdapter.h:40:69: error: no matching function for call to 'galsim::ImageView<double>::ImageView(double*&, const boost::shared_ptr<double>&, const int&, const galsim::Bounds<int>&)'
```
```
include/lsst/meas/extensions/shapeHSM/HsmAdapter.h:40:69: error: no matching function for call to 'galsim::ImageView<int>::ImageView(int*&, const boost::shared_ptr<int>&, const int&, const galsim::Bounds<int>&)'
```
```
include/lsst/meas/extensions/shapeHSM/HsmAdapter.h:40:69: error: no matching function for call to 'galsim::ImageView<int>::ImageView(int*&, const boost::shared_ptr<int>&, const int&, const galsim::Bounds<int>&)'

Technically, this is our (LSST's) problem, but none of the relevant developers have time to devote to fixing it, and that is going to interfere with DESC's ability to run ImSim with an updated GalSim on the upcoming data challenge. Thanks.

issue_title: Make organization a mandatory field while creating a project
issue_body:
On UI, organization is a mandatory field for a project. You can't save the project without assigning it a organization. But tower-cli allows to create a project without organization, which in turn messes up the permission on the job templates based on that project.

You can create a project like following:

~~~
tower-cli project create -n tower-cli-project --scm-type manual --local-path test_tower_cli
~~~

This will create a project names tower-cli-project on Tower without any organization.

After this create a job template based on above project project:

~~~
tower-cli job_template create -n test-tower-cli --project tower-cli-project --playbook test_module.yml --ask-inventory-on-launch true --ask-credential-on-launch true
~~~

This creates a job template named test-tower-cli on Tower. But if you check the permission tab, there is only a Admin permission for the user who created it. All the other System Admins will not show up there. 

This is because there is no organization in the Project. Once I put an organization in the project and recreate the job templates from tower-cli, I see the right permissions.

issue_title: "connect_address" parameter and replication
issue_body:
I need to provide a client connections via a pgbouncer (port 6432).
`App--5000-->haproxy(+confd)--6432-->pgbouncer--5432-->PostgreSQL(master).`

And for replication nodes to use 5432 (without pgbouncer).
`PostgreSQL(master)<--5432--PostgreSQL(replica) `

patroni.yml:
.....
postgresql:
  **listen**: 10.128.64.136:5432
  **connect_address**: 10.128.64.136:**6432**
.....

postgresql log:
FATAL:  could not connect to the primary server: ERROR:  Unsupported startup parameter: replication

cat /var/lib/postgresql/9.4/main/recovery.conf
primary_slot_name = 'node3'
standby_mode = 'on'
recovery_target_timeline = 'latest'
primary_conninfo = 'user=replicator password=rep-pass host=10.128.64.131 port=**6**432 sslmode=prefer sslcompression=1 application_name=node3'

https://patroni.readthedocs.io/en/latest/SETTINGS.html
**connect_address**: IP address + port through which Postgres is accessible from other nodes and applications.

**listen**: IP address + port that Postgres listens to; must be accessible from other nodes in the cluster, if you’re **using streaming replication**. Multiple comma-separated addresses are permitted, as long as the port component is appended after to the last one with a colon, i.e. listen: 127.0.0.1,127.0.0.2:5432. Patroni will use the first address from this list to establish local connections to the PostgreSQL node.

---------------------------
Debian 9 (4.9.0-6-amd64)
postgresql-9.4.18
patroni version 1.4.4 (pip)
pgbouncer 1.8.1
haproxy 1.8.9
confd 0.16.0

issue_title: Ctrl+D doesn't close tab
issue_body: Ctrl+D would normally exit a shell and cause the tab to close.  In 0.7.1 this no longer happens.  Instead `exit` is entered to the shell but the tab doesn't close.  At that point it's then impossible to close the tab or quit Guake entirely requiring the process to be manually killed.

issue_title: Datetimefield is serialized to str
issue_body:
When I serialize this model

```class Well(models.Model):
    site            = models.ForeignKey(Site, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    name         = models.CharField(max_length=100, )
    start_date_time = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=False, auto_now_add=False, verbose_name='Date and time at which the well is deployed.')
```

with this serializer
```
class FileSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = Well
        fields = ('id', 'name', 'site', )
```
The resulting dataframe has a column `start_date_time` but this is a string rather than a datetime object. I work around this by using `transform_dataframe` and convert the column using `pandas.to_datetime` function. Is this the way to go or am I missing someting?

issue_title: Django 1.10 Middlewares compatibility
issue_body: The current [middlewares](django_toolkit/middlewares.py) are made for Django<=1.9. Django 1.0 has a [new interface for middlewares](https://docs.djangoproject.com/es/1.10/topics/http/middleware/), so we should start using the `django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin`.

issue_title: Redundant Edge in Service Map
issue_body:
I created a lambda calling itself recursively for 3 times.
The lambda is set to“Enable active tracing”, and called `patch_all()`
It uses `boto3` to send request.

### Code:
```py
import boto3
import json
import requests
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder
from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch_all

patch_all()
pymongo_aws_xray.patch()

client = boto3.client('lambda')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    action = event['action']
    return globals()[action](event, context)


# input event['i']
# output {'result': j}, where j = 1^2 + 2^2 + .. + i^2
def square_sum(event, context):
    i = event['i']
    if i <= 0:
        return {'result': 0}
    if i == 1:
        return {'result': 1}
    payload_str = json.dumps({'action': 'square_sum', 'i': i-1})
    payload_b = bytes(payload_str, 'utf-8')
    tailcall = client.invoke(FunctionName='Test-Xray-Caller',
                             InvocationType='RequestResponse',
                             Payload=payload_b)
    tailcall_result = json.loads(tailcall['Payload'].read())
    result = tailcall_result['result'] + i * i
    return {'result': result}

```
### Expected graph
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7023694/40712225-bcd3126c-642f-11e8-8dad-b7926e0dd325.png)


### Real graph
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7023694/40711801-b5d98b7c-642e-11e8-9c72-38251ac8bd1c.png)

### Thinkings

In my understanding, a "remote" service means X-Ray does not recognize what endpoint it is, e.g. a non-AWS service. A call to AWS Lambda, shall have no "remote" services, with only one clean edge from the caller AWS service to the callee AWS service.

This redundant edge occurs more than here: When I call from lambda to Elastic Beanstalk endpoint, it still has "remote" services. 

How do I get rid of them?

One is synchronized via a lock and other other is not . This may be problematic and error prone.

issue_title: When compiling on CUDA, unnecessary compute architectures are generated
issue_body:
On this line, we are specifying that CUDA should compile on a bunch of different computation architectures from `compute_30` co `compute_52`:
https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/dynet/CMakeLists.txt#L125

In most cases, we probably only need to compile on a single computing architecture, the most recent one supported by the `nvcc` compiler and GPU. Because compiling for different architectures takes a long time and is a significant time bottleneck in getting DyNet installed on GPU, it would be nice to fix this. I'm not sure how to do so though, so if someone knows how help would be appreciated.

issue_title: Example in docstring for `failed` is wrong
issue_body:
The example suggests that the matcher you pass in is matched against the exception value:
```
        error = RuntimeError('foo')
        fails_at_runtime = failed(Equals(error))
        deferred = defer.fail(error)
        assert_that(deferred, fails_at_runtime)
```
However, it is actually matched against the failure. You would need to do something like this instead:
```
        error = RuntimeError('foo')
        fails_at_runtime = failed(AfterPreprocessing(lambda f: f.value, Equals(error)))
        deferred = defer.fail(error)
        assert_that(deferred, fails_at_runtime)
```
This is somewhat of an uncomfortable idiom; maybe we need an extra helper here to ease things along?

issue_title: Decouple Transaction from Connection
issue_body:
After #509 is in, we need to decouple `Transaction` from `Connection`  and allow `Transaction` to inherit from `Batch`.

Things to be done
-   [ ] Remove non-RPC methods `save_entity()` and `delete_entities()` from `Connection` (one-by-one for easier to review PRs?). Keep functionality around that allows updating mutations.
-   [ ] Remove `Connection.mutation()`. (Only used by `save_entity()` and `delete_entities()`.)
-   [ ] Remove `Connection.transaction()` (the actual de-coupling)
-   [ ] Change `Transaction` to inherit from `Batch`
-   [ ] Replace `save_entity()` and `delete_entities()` with generic `put()` and `delete()` top-level methods.

@tseaver Feel free to re-arrange this bullets as you see fit.

----
For reference, RPC [methods][1] are:

- Lookup
- RunQuery
- BeginTransaction
- Commit
- Rollback
- AllocateIds

[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-datastore/blob/21e029b2657ebb15fef5429c90c4315326855fd1/proto/datastore_v1.proto#L573

issue_title: Do we need to add support for Firestore labels?
How to test gcloud applications
issue_body:
Hello,

I have seen there are some efforts to create a local environment for testing, such as [the datastore emulator](https://cloud.google.com/datastore/docs/tools/datastore-emulator), and [the pubsub emulator](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/emulators/pubsub/), but I haven't seen more or documentation on how to use them.

One of the main blocks for the development with gcloud AFAI see is that there is not an standard to setup a integration testing environment. Certainly the ability to transparently connect and authenticate to upstream services is nice, but in order to develop, in the teams I work in, we really need to have a local testing environment.

The library is easy to mock, but I would highly appreciate having an standard way for developers have their testing environment, and documentation on how to do it.

I understand this effort is under `gcloud beta emulators`, but I have failed to find a way to integrate them with this library.

issue_title: Issue when inserting Stackdriver sink identity in Bigquery dataset
issue_body:
Hello,

I was going through the [documentation](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/stable/logging-usage.html) for setting up a Stackdriver sink using the python API and had some issues so I decided to ask here if I did something wrong (I got the answer already in [SO](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/43688393/bigquery-not-accepting-stackdrivers-sink-writer-identity?noredirect=1#comment74430872_43688393) but I'm not sure what happened yet).

The code I used:

```
from google.cloud.logging.client import Client as lClient
from google.cloud.bigquery.client import Client as bqClient
from google.cloud.bigquery.dataset import AccessGrant

import os
os.environ['GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS'] = 'path_to_json.json'

lc = lClient()
bqc = bqClient()

ds = bqc.dataset('working_dataset')
acc = ds.access_grants
acc.append(AccessGrant('WRITER', 'groupByEmail', 'cloud-logs@system.gserviceaccount.com')) #this is the service account that is shown in our Exports tab in GCP.
ds.access_grants = acc
ds.update()

```

I followed the docs but then I got the error when trying to update Bigquery's dataset:

    NotFound: 404 Not found: Email cloud-logs@system.gserviceaccount.com (PUT https://www.googleapis.com/bigquery/v2/projects/[project-id]/datasets/[working-dataset])

As Xuejian helped me, this account is not a _group_ one but rather a _user_ one. I tried changing it but then got the error:

    BadRequest: 400 Cannot remove all owners from a dataset.

So I decided to create a brand new access_grants for my dataset object and this time I put all accounts related to it, something like:

```
acc = [AccessGrant('OWNER', 'userByEmail', 'owner who created dataset email'), AccessGrant('WRITER', 'userByEmail', 'cloud-logs@system.gserviceaccount.com')]
```

And this time it worked. So I was wondering, when we run `ds.access_grants` shouldn't it already bring all accesses related to this dataset? Also should the documentation be updated with **userByEmail** instead of **groupbyEmail** when creating the access of the sink's writer?

My setup was Python 3.6. from latest jessie docker image

Thanks,

issue_title: Unable to fetch transient dependency com.google.http-client:google-http-client:[1.19.0,2.0)
issue_body:
My app-engine project depends on this library (using gradle):
`compile com.google.appengine.tools:appengine-gcs-client:0.6`

But, when i try to compile project with command '`gradlew clean compileJava`' i got an error:
--------------
```
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
Could not resolve all dependencies for configuration ':compile'.
> Could not find any version that matches com.google.http-client:google-http-client:[1.19.0,2.0).
  Versions that do not match:
      1.5.3-beta
      1.5.0-beta
  Searched in the following locations:
      https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/com/google/http-client/google-http-client/maven-metadata.xml
  Required by:
      :my-project:unspecified > com.google.appengine.tools:appengine-gcs-client:0.6
```
--------------

That's happens because file in path `https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/com/google/http-client/google-http-client/maven-metadata.xml` really outdated and contains information about only two versions `1.5.0-beta` and `1.5.3-beta`.

issue_title: Bundling fails on rsync on (non-GCE) CentOS 6.5
issue_body:
I'm trying to bundle up Cent OS 6.5 created inside Windows Azure to move it to GCE.

The bundle operation fails with 
```
19:14:34 platform exclude list: (/etc/ssh/.host_key_regenerated, 0:0:0) (/dev, 0:1:0) (/proc, 0:1:0) (/run, 0:1:1) (/selinux, 0:0:0) (/tmp, 0:1:0) (/sys, 0:1:0) (/var/lib/google/per-instance, 0:1:0) (/var/lock, 0:1:1) (/var/log, 0:1:1) (/var/run, 0:1:1)
19:14:34 ignoring mounts /proc /sys /dev/pts /dev/shm /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc /mnt/resource
19:14:34 overwrite list = 
19:14:34 Initializing disk file
19:14:43 .
19:14:45 Making filesystem
19:14:58 .
19:15:13 .
19:15:21 Copying contents
19:15:21 Error while running ['rsync', '--times', '--perms', '--owner', '--group', '--links', '--devices', '--acls', '--sparse', '--hard-links', '--recursive', '--xattrs', u'--exclude-from=/tmp/tmpzSPO9Z', '/', u'/tmp/tmpaBbUgv'] return_code = 11
19:15:21 stdout=
19:15:21 stderr=rsync: failed to open exclude file /tmp/tmpzSPO9Z: Permission denied (13)
19:15:21 rsync error: error in file IO (code 11) at exclude.c(1062) [client=3.0.6]
```

After searching forums, doing research, I've found the reason is SElinux forbidding to access temp files.

Stopping SELinux by 
```
echo 0 > /selinux/enforce
```
fixed the issue.

The problem appeared on CentOS at Windows Azure but it can appear on GCE too if SELinux is on and configured to prevent reading from temp files. 
Adding the note to README or even adding turning off selinux before running rsync will solve the issue.

Regards,
Vladimir

issue_title: Bootstrap not being used after install
issue_body:
First of all, I am a real noobie when it comes to ruby on rails, so please bear with my lack of experience.

It comes regarding the install tutorial for ruby on rails. At one moment it says 

> Make sure the file has .scss extension (or .sass for Sass syntax). If you have just generated a new Rails app, it may come with a .css file instead. If this file exists, it will be served instead of Sass, so rename it:

issue_title: acquire_token not thread-safe
issue_body:
The acquire_token method is not thread-safe.

Example:
```
context = adal.AuthenticationContext(authority, api_version=None)

context.acquire_token_with_username_password(resource, username, password, client_id)

# Then executing the method below in parallell when the token cache expires, say with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor will cause a KeyError. 

context.acquire_token(resource, username, client_id)

# The issue seems to be a dict.pop operation during cache update which itself is not thread-safe.
```

issue_title: The `--pre` option is not passed from command-line options down to API call
issue_body:
When a `--pre` option is used from command line it is stored on an instance of `ResolverOptionsBuilder` only, but that instance is not passed to the call to `resolve_multi()`.
Neither is its `_allow_prerelease` attribute passed to the keyword argument of `resolve_multi()`.
This results in the creation of a new `ResolverOptionsBuilder` instance in `resolve()` (called from `resolve_multi()`) and the exclusion of pre-release packages despite the `--pre` command-line option.

The specific case where this hidden behavior was discovered requires a dependency at a specific version (e.g., `dep==1.2.3b`), while another package requires the same dependency with no version specified (e.g., `dep`). The resulting error from pex is:
```
Could not satisfy all requirements for dep==1.2.3b1:
    dep==1.2.3b1, dep
```

I have a fix for this ready and will send a PR.

% ls -l /usr/bin/python3
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 9 Dec  1 00:12 /usr/bin/python3 -> python3.4*
% pex -v --python=/usr/bin/python3 -o world.pex -r world --no-wheel -e worldlib.__main__:main
pex: Resolving distributions: 517.2ms          
pex:     Packaging world: 354.3ms
  world 3.1
Setting entry point to worldlib.__main__:main
Saving PEX file to world.pex
% head -1 world.pex
#!/usr/bin/env python3.4
```

It doesn't seem to matter if ```--python=/usr/bin/python3.4``` is used (i.e. it's not related to python3 being a symlink).

issue_title: Ensure that JavacPluginSetup is set up in tests
issue_body:
Right now there's a hack in place to allow code to work even if that subsystem isn't set up. Without the hack, hundreds of tests that rely on JvmTarget fail.

It could maybe be fixed by transitively loading all subsystems required by the Targets in BuildFileAliases in TaskTestBase.

issue_title: fix has_native_sources to check requirement platforms, not source filenames
issue_body: We introduced the `PythonNativeCode` subsystem in #5815, which checks whether to build for the current platform only by checking if any `python_dist()` or `ctypes_compatible_c/cpp_library()` targets have any native sources. This is totally wrong -- this should test whether the closure of python requirements (including local `python_dist()`s) has any platform-specific packages. This would allow us to remove the `native_source_extensions` option in that subsystem.

issue_title: check {deprecated,removed}_version for config settings
issue_body:
When an option is renamed, one might leave an option with the old name around and set it deprecated/removed, to communicate to consumers what happened/is going to happen to it.

pants currently only checks deprecated_version/removed_version for values that come from args, so values in configs won't trigger these warnings/errors, and pants.ini maintainers will be unaware they're using an option that's deprecated/removed.

issue_title: Pinger task in the artifact cache is not configurable, ignores configured caches silently
issue_body:
I am diagnosing some artifact cache issues. I noticed that the pinger task in cache_setup.py has no configuration options.  

```
    self._pinger = pinger or Pinger(timeout=0.5, tries=2)
```

The fix for this would be to add option settings above in register_options()

There seem to be no visible diagnostics (without turning on debugging) if the pinger task fails and pants runs as if no artifact caches are visible.  The fix for this would be to print a warning to the console that no configured caches are available.  

I would argue that printing this as a warning is justified because if you have an unavailable cache configured, you are wasting .5 seconds trying to contact it every time you run pants.

issue_title: config verifier trips up on DEFAULT configs inherited by deprecated options
issue_body:
Apply the following patch diff, which represents moving the checkstyle task from the compile goal to the lint goal, at a6d90633c7:

```
diff --git a/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py b/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
index 106aa0763..828af8137 100644
--- a/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
+++ b/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
@@ -11,5 +11,5 @@ from pants.goal.task_registrar import TaskRegistrar as task
 
 
 def register_goals():
-  task(name='checkstyle', action=Checkstyle).install('compile')
+  task(name='checkstyle', action=Checkstyle).install('lint')
   task(name='scalastyle', action=Scalastyle).install('compile')
diff --git a/pants.ini b/pants.ini
index 0f4cab06f..f7e9f0a44 100644
--- a/pants.ini
+++ b/pants.ini
@@ -129,7 +129,7 @@ deps: ["3rdparty/python:thrift"]
 antlr3_deps: ["3rdparty/python:antlr-3.1.3"]
 
 
-[compile.checkstyle]
+[lint.checkstyle]
 configuration: %(pants_supportdir)s/checkstyle/coding_style.xml
 
 [compile.errorprone]
diff --git a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
index 872eab91b..041a32c5a 100644
--- a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
+++ b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
@@ -23,6 +23,8 @@ class Checkstyle(NailgunTask):
 
   :API: public
   """
+  deprecated_options_scope = 'compile.checkstyle'
+  deprecated_options_scope_removal_version = '9.9.9.dev0'
 
   _CHECKSTYLE_MAIN = 'com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.Main'
```

Now:

```
gonzo:[~/src/pants][master]$ ./pants
WARN] /Users/benjyw/src/pants/src/python/pants/option/options_bootstrapper.py:171: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: scope compile.checkstyle will be removed in version 9.9.9.dev0.
  Use scope lint.checkstyle instead (options: jvm_options)
  valid_options_under_scope = set(options.for_scope(scope))

No goals specified.
Use `pants goals` to list goals.
Use `pants help` to get help.
```

Whereas:

```
gonzo:[~/src/pants][master]$ ./pants --no-verify-config
No goals specified.
Use `pants goals` to list goals.
Use `pants help` to get help.
```

What's happening is that we have `jvm_options` in the `DEFAULT` section of our pants.ini, and the verifier sees this inherited into the deprecated scope. But obviously this doesn't represent a true use of deprecated options, so we shouldn't display this message in this case.

Where is a simple method that just does all this and returns you a text?

issue_title: write_gif() tries to use an invalid quantization algorithm 'wu'
issue_labels: [{'id': 143381070, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/imageio/imageio/labels/plugin:%20freeimage', 'name': 'plugin: freeimage', 'color': 'c7def8', 'default': False}, {'id': 495624726, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/imageio/imageio/labels/plugin:%20pillow', 'name': 'plugin: pillow', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 223609055, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/imageio/imageio/labels/type:%20task', 'name': 'type: task', 'color': '6339a0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
When calling `moviepy.video.VideoClip.write_gif()`, the function `converToPIL()` in [`imageio/plugins/pillowmulti.py`](../../imageio/blob/master/imageio/plugins/pillowmulti.py) is given a value of `'wu'` for the quantizer. This results in the following error:

```python
/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/imageio/plugins/pillowmulti.py in converToPIL(self, im, quantizer, palette_size)
    348             im_pil = im_pil.quantize(colors=palette_size, method=quantizer)
    349         else:
--> 350             raise ValueError('Invalid value for quantizer: %r' % quantizer)
    351         return im_pil

ValueError: Invalid value for quantizer: 'wu'

```

This happens even when, for example, `clip.write_gif('myclip.gif', opt=0)` is given. (giving `opt='nq'` actaully sticks, but fails earlier in the same if-statement.) 

I couldn't find where `quantizer` was being set to `'wu'`, but was able to work around this by placing 
```python
if quantizer == 'wu':
    quantizer = 0
```
immediately before the offending if-statement. 

I'm not a software developer, but it seems like this method should default to using `0` for the quantizer until `'wu'` is implemented.

issue_title: File list passed into a ContentNode must consist of more than just paths
issue_body:
So far, we've gotten away with passing in a list of simple string paths as `files` when instantiating `ContentNode` subclasses. E.g. `Document(files=["http://mysite.com/somedoc.pdf"])`. It uses the class' `default_preset` to determine the preset for the file(s) in the list.

This won't be enough for many contexts. For example, once we start adding in subtitles, we'll need to indicate, for each of the subtitle files, what language it's for. We may also want to pass in options regarding caching, preprocessing, etc for each file.

I would propose having a `File` class, which takes a `path` (URL or file path), as well as an optional `preset` (or let it autodetect that, if desired). If appropriate to the preset, settings like `language` could also be passed in.

There could then also be subclasses of `File` that provide additional options, e.g. `VideoFile` that encapsulates the logic for doing resizing and other preprocessing.

Tokenizer not unconsuming X in in hex mode
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm implementing the WHATWG spec with C++/Qt and using your implementation as a reference is quite useful.
But I think I just found an error, 
in tokenizer.py line 172 there is:
self.stream.unget(charStack.pop())
sorry if my Python knowledge is mistaken but here
you only pop one char from the stack but in a '#Xabc' case
where the X stands for hexadecimals you sould
pop that too as the docs says: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/syntax.html#tokenizing-character-references
"If no characters match the range, then don't consume any characters (and unconsume the U+0023 NUMBER SIGN character and, if appropriate, the X character). This is a parse error; nothing is returned."
Best.

issue_title: Modifying object attributes that are dictionaries may not save updates
issue_body:
If you modify a dictionary on an APIResource and then save() the resource, the updates are not sent. This is because  [__setitem__ is not getting called on the resource](https://github.com/solvebio/solvebio-python/blob/35044c3303e84a8ee7e08a8a8ee6184080b0e668/solvebio/resource/solveobject.py#L63) in this case and not updating the `_unsaved_values` set.

Example
```
# Failure case
print obj.metadata
{}
obj.metadata['foo'] = 'bar'
obj.save()
# nada
print obj.metadata
{}

# Workaround
obj.metadata = {'foo': 'bar'}
obj.save()
print obj.metadata
{'foo': 'bar'}
```

issue_title: Mismatch between docs and behavior of PingCollector
issue_body:
Suppose `PingCollector` is configured with
```
target_website = 'www.example.org'
```

The documentation ("We extract out the key after target_ and use it in the graphite node we push.") suggests that the metric sent will be `servers.hostname.ping.website`, but the actual metric sent is `servers.hostname.ping.www_example_org`. I don't know what the intended behavior is (and thus whether the doc or the code is wrong), only that there is a mismatch.

issue_title: PortStat - Counter not available in 2.6
issue_body:
`from collections import Counter` from [here](https://github.com/python-diamond/Diamond/blob/master/src/collectors/portstat/portstat.py#L10) does not work in python 2.6 as it was implemented in 2.7

A backported version of Counter can be located [here](http://code.activestate.com/recipes/576611-counter-class/).  It might be good to include this somewhere in the project as a fallback in case the collections.Counter can't be loaded.

issue_title: Diamond does not set groups properly when changing uid and gid
issue_body:
According to [Python issue 10032](http://bugs.python.org/issue10032), setting the `uid` and `gid` is not enough to simulate logging in as that user. The `os.initgroups()` function needs to be used to initialize the groups of the user being changed to, like this:

```python
    if gid != -1 and uid != -1:
        os.initgroups(pwd.getpwuid(uid).pw_name, gid)
```

If this is not done, Diamond can't invoke actions on files when it changes to a user that only has group permissions for those files, since Python doesn't know that it's a member of those groups.

issue_title: Curly braces in Procfile are unescaped
issue_body:
The Deis scheduler handling of Dockerfile + Procfile is now broken.

We had deployed a cluster with Deis v1.0.1 following the guidance in [gh#2545](https://github.com/deis/deis/issues/2545) for the Dockerfile + Procfile behavior at the time.

For that, we had the Dockerfile CMD start a script that prepares application environment variables. If it detects $1 as the proctype ("start web" would pass "web", for example) and would run foreman start on that proctype, otherwise it would execute $@ outright.

This was logical. The Dockerfile had a CMD, and Deis honored it. It wasn't pretty, but it wasn't horrible either.

We just deployed a cluster with Deis v1.3.1, which no longer honors the CMD script defined in the Dockerfile.

Instead, it seems that [pr2605](https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/2605) has the scheduler scanning the procfile to find the command to run.

This is conceptually the correct behavior. However, the generated unit isn't being executed in a pure shell context.

Case in point, here's our Procfile:

    web: bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload

And here is the unit that Deis v1.3.1's fleet scheduler now generates:

    [Unit]
    Description=myapp_v5.web.1
    
    [Service]
    ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/,myapp:v5; docker pull $IMAGE"
    ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "docker inspect myapp_v5.web.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker rm -f myapp_v5.web.1 || true"
    ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/myapp:v5; port=$(docker inspect -f '{{range $k, $v := .ContainerConfig.ExposedPorts }}{{$k}}{{end}}' $IMAGE | cut -d/ -f1) ; docker run --name myapp.web.1 -m 3g   -P -e PORT=$port $IMAGE bash -c 'bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload'"
    ExecStop=/usr/bin/docker rm -f myapp_v5.web.1
    TimeoutStartSec=20m
    RestartSec=5
    Restart=on-failure

The shell script quoting looks right. But this is a systemd service. As we all know from painful first-hand experience, those variable expansions happen differently there.

When I look at the running process that is failing to start using ps, I see this:

    docker run --name myapp.web.1 -m 3g -P -e PORT=3000 10.113.1.220:5000/myapp:v5 bash -c bundle exec puma -t : -w  -p  -e  --preload

That's right. All of those ${} expansions are being eaten by systemd.

My problem here is that now I can't simply backslash the $'s in the Procfile and not break my other environments (nor should I have to).

I still have this stuff running on developer workstations using docker-expose/fig, which correctly runs the CMD entrypoint script of the Dockerfile to start the container there.

I also have this deployed to that old deis v1.0.1 cluster, which likewise would break if I do the same.

Instead, I now have to change my Procfile to look like this:

    web: ./bin/web.sh

And in bin/web.sh in the project I now have this:

    #!/bin/bash
    set -eo pipefail
    
    # re-export the environment variables for the next process
    export $(env | cut -d= -f1)
    
    proc="exec bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload"
    
    if [[ -x /run.sh ]]; then
      /run.sh "$proc"
    else
      $proc
    fi

This "hides" those variables from systemd and lets them evaluate in a shell context as they should be.

It would be great if we could default to running the Dockerfile CMD by default as it has been in the past, but allow that to be overridden by a later deis client command for a given app and proctype if the devops so desires.

As mentioned above, I've worked around this. No doubt others WILL encounter this problem, and this WILL be a support issue for someone other than myself, it's just a matter of time.

You've already partially reverted this behavior at least for heroku buildpacks in [gh#2673](https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/2673).

What does OpDemand think?

issue_title: Deis upgrade client
issue_body:
The first phase of the Deis update service (#2106) is an update client which is called when a Deis platform upgrade is available. Whether this is implemented as a command in `deisctl` (like `deisctl upgrade`) or as a separate client is yet to be determined.

This client will have the necessary logic to perform a zero-downtime upgrade of Deis components to a new release version.

issue_title: tar command fails inside docker for Java buildpack
issue_body:
I'm seeing similar reports for Meteor buildpack to docker and coreos (https://github.com/docker/docker/issues/19647, https://github.com/coreos/bugs/issues/1095), and so I'm not sure if i should be reporting this there or here, but at any rate:

As of the last couple of weeks, when I attempt to push a Java-based application (which had successfully deployed on deis previousily), I am now getting

tar: jre/lib/amd64/server: Directory renamed before its status could be extracted
tar: jre/lib/amd64: Directory renamed before its status could be extracted
tar: jre/lib: Directory renamed before its status could be extracted
tar: jre: Directory renamed before its status could be extracted
tar: Exiting with failure status due to previous errors

Doing a little poking around, It's failing on the command `tar pxzf /tmp/build/.jdk/jdk.tar.gz -C /tmp/build/.jdk` which gets called by the Heroku java buildpack

Bottom line: should I provide more details on deis/docker/core/etc. versions or should I be lookup upstream (i.e. to docker or core os)?  The bug reports I linked to above suggest it was related to some overlayfs but I don't know why that would suddenly change since I haven't knowingly updated deis packages etc. at the point when stuff started breaking.

issue_title: Globus Connect Personal UUID
issue_body:
GCP has a file containing the UUID of its endpoint. Unix installs it goes in `~/.globusonline/lta/client-id.txt` and on Windows it goes in `%LocalAppData%\Globus Connect\client-id.txt`.

So we do have the ability to provide a command to show the UUID to a user which I think we certainly want to do.  The question is really, should this just be implemented directly in the CLI or should we also add SDK methods?

issue_title: Add manpages for all commands
issue_body:
`--help` documentation for async-transfer has already started to run up against the limits of simple inline help.
The helpdoc is really long, and it's hard to document the `--batch` behavior in there.

I didn't expect to come up against this so soon, but I think we already need man pages.

Likely this will be sphinx autodoc fed back into the CLI as a set of commands.
My personal tendency at present is to add `--man` as a global option that behaves much like the `aws help` subcommands.

issue_title: rm missing
issue_body: The ssh CLI has an "rm" command that emulates the Unix rm.  It supports file globbing, and blocks waiting for completion.  The new CLI should have this command also.

issue_title: Windows - libethash - multiple concurrent miners crash application
Use `__slots__`
issue_body:
http://book.pythontips.com/en/latest/__slots__magic.html

Currently we use this pattern to denote that `MyThing` will have a `prop_a` property.

```python
class MyThing:
    prop_a = None
```

It looks like if we instead use `__slots__` we could see 40-50% reduction in memory footprint.

```python
class MyThing:

issue_title: Why forcing DatetimeField to utcnow() ?
issue_body:
Hi!

We've started using your library @komoot and we've rapidly hit the problem that it is impossible to give a ``None`` value to a ``DateTimeField`` in schema mode.

This part of the code: https://github.com/djangonauts/django-hstore/blob/c5d9b46fa6a678e864c32ed29ee63e37f26df3ca/django_hstore/virtual.py#L102

Seems to indicate that any default value set to ``None`` will be converted to ``datetime.utcnow()``. Is there a reason for this specific behavior? If I'm not mistaken django ORM authorizes ``None`` values for ``DateTimeField`` if ``null=True``.

Our workaround for now is to set ``'kwargs': {'default': ''}``, however it forces our schema to be in a quiet inconsistent state...

Thanks for your explanation :-)

The traceback is as follows:

  File "C:\Program Files\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\conda\base\context.py", line 561, in <module>

  File "C:\Program Files\Anaconda2\lib\_abcoll.py", line 571, in update
    for key, value in other:
  File "C:\Program Files\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\conda\common\configuration.py", line 367, in <genexpr>
    raw_data = odict(kv for kv in chain.from_iterable(load_paths))

issue_title: Provide a conda command to install dependencies from package metadata?
issue_body:
Imagine I'm developing 3 pure python packages. In the python world, I might run `pip install -e .` on each of the 3 git checkouts, which would install the 3 packages in 'develop'/'editable' mode, and would also install the dependencies of all 3 into my current environment.

Often, I would prefer to use conda instead of pip - particularly for installing the dependencies of the python packages I'm developing. The python packages I'm developing always have a meta.yaml file in the repository (for building a conda package to release), so I hoped I would be able to use that somehow via `conda develop`. However, `conda develop` does not seem to do what I want, and is currently not recommended (e.g. https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/2633#issuecomment-359572215, https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1990#issuecomment-305549938, https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1992).

A suggestion that seems to have come up several times for `conda develop` is that it should create an environment from the meta.yaml file (e.g. https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1677), in addition to installing the package in 'develop' mode. However, I would prefer a new environment not to be created (or for it to be optional), because I want to be able to install multiple packages' sets of dependencies into the same, existing environment. I would also have no problem running `pip install --no-deps -e` myself, after having installed the dependencies using conda, because I am only ever working on pure python packages<sup>1</sup>.

I think what I want has been asked before (#5126), but the resolution there is not what I'm looking for. Unless I'm misunderstanding, I think that issue leads to a command to do something like `conda install --dependencies-only pkg`, where pkg is an existing (built) conda package. I don't want to have to build the conda package and then install dependencies from it because that's a time consuming process - particularly if I am currently editing the dependencies - and the package could anyway fail to be created (e.g. my package tests might fail). And I don't want to use an existing version of pkg from elsewhere because the dependencies may be out of date.

Currently, projects I see tend to have developer instructions like one of the following:

  * `conda install pkg && conda remove --force pkg && pip install --no-deps -e .` (which gets a developer the released package's dependencies, not those specified in the repo's current meta.yaml file), or

  * `conda install dep1 dep2 ... depN && pip install --no-deps -e .` where dep1, dep2, ..., depN are somehow never quite the same as what's in meta.yaml (accidentally, not just because dependencies have moved on since last time), and are different from what developers actually install, and vary again on travis and appveyor too (accidentally, not just because there are genuine platform differences).

A `conda "install dependencies from" meta.yaml` command could cut down the times dependencies are specified in a project, greatly improving the chance that developers and CI systems are all using the same dependencies, which will be the same ones as in the conda package when it's released, helping users get a reliable package.

I guess I'm hoping that by asking for only an "install dependencies from meta.yaml" command rather than to fix/upgrade `conda develop`, there might somehow be less work involved. It naively looks to me like I could put together some kind of command to get the dependencies from meta.yaml using `conda_build.api.render()` and then reading `'requirements/run'` etc, but there are probably subtleties I'm not considering! 

-----

1. I don't know if `conda develop` is supposed to be python only, or if it's expected to do any more of what's specified in a package's meta.yaml/associated build scripts. I haven't thought about that because I'm biased to developing only python packages.

issue_title: Conda gives "Malformed version string" for versions with wildcard after underscore in 4.2.0
issue_body:
In Anaconda 4.1, I could specify package dependency versions in meta.yaml as:
`- package.name sometag_*`

In 4.2.0, I get an error like this from the `VersionSpec` class when running conda-build:
`conda.exceptions.CondaValueError: Value error: Malformed version string 'sometag_': empty version component.`

The same thing happens with `conda install package.name=sometag_*`. In both cases, `sometag_*` has to be changed to `sometag*`, which seems like a bug (why should the expression fail to match only when the wildcard appears after a certain character in the full version string?).

This is no big deal for me, since I can just omit the underscore, but in principle doing so could lead to ambiguity without that delimiter, where one tag is a substring of another, which would be a more serious problem. I would also like to be able to give internal users instructions including the underscore.

No urgency, mainly just FYI. Thanks!

James.

issue_title: Mirroring the pip sub-package (extras) dependency semantics
issue_body:
With pip one can `pip install ipython[all]` to get all optional packages listed under the [`ipython` extras](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/master/setup.py#L182).

AFAIC, this interface is not currently possible with conda (apologies if it is), and I believe it would make an excellent addition.

This would open the door to making optional sub-packages easily conda installable and would  be extremely useful for ipython and matplotlib, to name a few.
