issue_title: Problem appending strings to a bcolz array
issue_body:
When appending strings to a bcolz array with the following code:

    bz = bcolz.carray(np.array([], dtype="S500"))
    for i in range(1000):
        bz.append(np.array(["xxx"]))
        if len(bz) >= 2 and type(bz[1]) == np.ndarray:

issue_title: `get_cigar_stats` documentation is inaccurate since support for B-tag was added
issue_body:
The `AlignedSegment.get_cigar_stats` method returns arrays of length 11, not length 10 as implied by the docstring.

The discrepancy is due to the addition of support for a "B" cigar tag that has not been reflected in the docstring (see e6c9bda, although the release notes suggest that the B tag has been recognised since pysam version 0.7).

The `AlignedSegment.get_cigar_stats` is as follows:

```
        """summary of operations in cigar string.
        The output order in the array is "MIDNSHP=X" followed by a
        field for the NM tag. If the NM tag is not present, this
        field will always be 0.
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |M    |BAM_CMATCH    |0    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |I    |BAM_CINS      |1    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |D    |BAM_CDEL      |2    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |N    |BAM_CREF_SKIP |3    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |S    |BAM_CSOFT_CLIP|4    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |H    |BAM_CHARD_CLIP|5    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |P    |BAM_CPAD      |6    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |=    |BAM_CEQUAL    |7    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |X    |BAM_CDIFF     |8    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |NM   |NM tag        |9    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        If no cigar string is present, empty arrays will be returned.
        Parameters
        ----------
        Returns
        -------
        arrays : two arrays. The first contains the nucleotide counts within
           each cigar operation, the second contains the number of blocks for
           each cigar operation.
        """
```

I propose this be ammended to something like:

```
        """summary of operations in cigar string.
        The output order in the array is "MIDNSHP=XB" followed by a
        field for the NM tag. If the NM tag is not present, this
        field will always be 0.
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |M    |BAM_CMATCH    |0    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |I    |BAM_CINS      |1    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |D    |BAM_CDEL      |2    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |N    |BAM_CREF_SKIP |3    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+

issue_title: wishbone master: strobe/stall/ack probabilities
issue_body:
Hi

While trying to fix the wishbone master command order, I tried to also verify the time required by each command in the testbench.
A proposal for this is here:
https://github.com/eschmidscs/vunit/tree/wbm_time

However, there are two general issues with the current probabilities:
First, they are mathematically unbound. It is possible that the master stalls forever.
Second, the stall should IMHO not be a probability but a queue length. The probability that the queue stalls is given by the ack probability of the slave. If both are probabilities, this becomes extremely hard to verify.

So I propose that the strobe and ack probabilities should rather be the maximum wait cycles for the random generator (i.e. use some rand(0, MAX) to get the number of wait cycles). Further, the stall probability should rather be a queue length.

Currently, only the wishbone and avalon VCs seem to have these mechanisms (thanks, Slawomir). Are there any plans for the other VCs in this direction?

issue_title: Implicit HTTP port violates scheme default
issue_body:
The `elasticsearch.Elasticsearch` class treats members of the `hosts` argument list in a way that fails to conform to [RFC-3986, Section 3.2.3](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-3.2.3). A URL that does not specify a port should use the port that is the default for the _scheme_, which is "http" in this case. The scheme's default port may be overridden by a local cache of the [services](https://www.iana.org/assignments/service-names-port-numbers/service-names-port-numbers.xhtml) registry, typically `/etc/services` or `C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\services`.

### Expected behavior

```
>>> import elasticsearch
>>> elasticsearch.VERSION
(6, 1, 0, 'dev')
>>> es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch("http://localhost")
>>> es.__dict__['transport'].__dict__['seed_connections']
(<Urllib3HttpConnection: http://localhost:80>,)
>>>
```

### Actual behavior

```
>>> import elasticsearch
>>> elasticsearch.VERSION
(6, 1, 0, 'dev')
>>> es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch("http://localhost")
>>> es.__dict__['transport'].__dict__['seed_connections']
(<Urllib3HttpConnection: http://localhost:9200>,)
>>>
```

issue_title: Frequent Connection Reset errors with 0.0.17
issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**
We recently upgraded to version 0.0.17 and have been experiencing a multitude of "Connection Reset" errors when interacting with ADLS.

Example error:

`DatalakeRESTException: HTTP error: ConnectionError(ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')),)`

---

### Reproduction Steps
** Enumerate the steps to reproduce the issue here:**
1. Authenticate to ADLS
2. Get a list of files from a directory listing
3. Iterate over file list
4.  Open each file into a pandas dataframe, e.g.
    `df = pd.read_csv(adl.open(file_))`

The errors are frequent but occur on separate files, i.e. it's not reproducible on an individual file basis.  We're seeing the error 60-70% of the time when reading files in a directory listing.

As a mitigation step, I downgraded azure-datalake-store to 0.0.16, and the issue improved dramatically.  Now seeing the Connection Reset error perhaps <5% of the time.

### Environment summary

**SDK Version:** What version of the SDK  are you using? (pip show azure-datalake-store)
Answer here:
0.0.17

**Python Version:** What Python version are you using? Is it 64-bit or 32-bit?  
Answer here:
Python 3.6.1 :: Anaconda 4.4.0 (64-bit)

**OS Version:** What OS and version are you using?

Ubuntu Linux 16.04

Jupyter notebook/bash on Ubuntu Linux

issue_title: Ability to denote version requirements in hooks.yaml for Ruby dependencies [Feature Request]
issue_body:
Right now it's not possible to note which version of a gem should be installed in the additional dependencies because of the way that inputs into the gem install command are shellescaped.

https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/blob/master/pre_commit/languages/ruby.py#L102

Ex: Trying to install version 5.0.0 of the foodcritic gem results in this `gem install --no-ri --no-rdoc 'foodcritic --version 5.0.0'`

It would be great to be able to mark a gem and a version to be installed in the hooks.yaml rather than having to create an empty gemspec and manage them that way.

issue_title: Null Space Inverse Kinematics has problems with fixed joints
issue_body:
I believe the issue found and fixed in Inverse Dynamics with fixed joints (https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/issues/1379) may also be present in Null Space Inverse Kinematics.

In particular, `lowerLimits`, `upperLimits`, `jointRanges`, `restPoses` are expected to have lengths equal to the number of joints by `calculateInverseKinematics` : see e.g.  https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/master/examples/pybullet/pybullet.c#L8120

But once passed to bullet, it looks like the number of dofs is expected instead: e.g. https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/abeae7e1e7e23d5ba8e840e98b46470d4162a73b/examples/SharedMemory/PhysicsClientC_API.cpp#L4001

Unfortunately padding the arrays until they are length numJoints (as suggested in the previous issue) doesn't seem to fix this one.

I am still finding my way around the code base so apologies if I've read this wrong. Thanks again for your time and help!

issue_title: UnicodeDecodeError when deserializing DynamoDB response
issue_body:
## Problem

DynamoDB::Query operations fail due to a `UnicodeDecodeError` during response deserialization:
```
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x8b in position 1: invalid start byte
```

An identical query succeeds using regular botocore.

## Discussion

0x8b (139) is outside of the ASCII range and based on some cursory Googling, appears to indicate that the response is gzipped:

* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11072705/twitter-trends-api-unicodedecodeerror-utf8-codec-cant-decode-byte-0x8b-in-po
* https://github.com/rg3/youtube-dl/issues/366
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9445627/urllib2-opener-providing-wrong-charset

Related to #258?

## Minimal reproducer

```python
import asyncio
from pprint import pprint

import aiobotocore
import botocore.session


QUERY_KWARGS = {
    'ExpressionAttributeNames': {
        '#key': 'key',
        '#seq': 'seq',
        '#stream': 'stream',
        '#timestamp': 'timestamp',
        '#value': 'value',
    },
    'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
        ':stream': {'S': 'channel/1139320d29cb42e9813be24d363bc464/content'},
    },
    'IndexName': 'seq',
    'KeyConditionExpression': '#stream = :stream',
    'ProjectionExpression': '#seq, #key, #value, #timestamp',
    'ScanIndexForward': False,
    'TableName': 'flow-orbit-stage-events',
}


def query_botocore():
    session = botocore.session.get_session()

    ddb = session.create_client('dynamodb')
    paginator = ddb.get_paginator('query')
    page_iterator = paginator.paginate(**QUERY_KWARGS)

    for page in page_iterator:
        pprint(page)


async def query_aiobotocore():
    session = aiobotocore.get_session()

    async with session.create_client('dynamodb') as ddb:
        paginator = ddb.get_paginator('query')
        page_iterator = paginator.paginate(**QUERY_KWARGS)

        async for page in page_iterator:
            pprint(page)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    print('Running query with vanilla botocore')
    query_botocore()

    print()

    print('Running query with aiobotocore')
    asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(query_aiobotocore())
```

I upgraded to IPython 4.0.0 and (correspondingly) now use `jupyter notebook` to work with my Notebooks. However, I find that my Markdown cells with LaTeX `\newcommand` no longer seems to set up a reusable macro. (This was absolutely fine in IPython 3.4 from which I had migrated.) What am I missing? Or, is this some bug? Any help would be appreciated! Thanks!

Here's a very simple Notebook markdown and its output.

````$\newcommand{innerproduct}[2]{\langle #1, #2 \rangle}$````

![jupyternb_nwcmmnd_mnwe](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5288070/9505329/537f16f0-4c07-11e5-9370-9cd1763471b0.png)

issue_title: Functionality to import from other notebooks or child notebooks
issue_body:
We can import functions from one python file to another. We should be able to do the same for notebooks. 
I have very long notebook and it becomes tough to scroll through it. Ideally I would like to create small notebooks each having separate and independent functionality and then I should be able to import these child notebooks in master notebook.

issue_title: Issue with comparison with cleverhans
issue_body:
I've been using foolbox for quite some time and to be thorough I've been doing comparisons with cleverhans, since that's what a lot of people in the community are going to be testing with. 

I've noticed an issue, particularly with FGSM where cleverhans is somehow mounting a much stronger attack. On reviewing both implementations I can't find a suitable difference. 

If I load identical models and run seemingly identical FGSM attack scripts on MNIST with an epsilon of 0.3. 
I get these results:
```
test_foolbox.py
Clean accuracy: 0.998%
Attack success: 0.477%
Average Linf norm: 0.300000011921
```

```
test_tf.py
Clean accuracy: 0.9980
Adversarial accuracy: 0.2160
```

I created a test repo with two files, one for cleverhans, and one for foolbox, using the same keras model def and weights [here]. (https://github.com/neale/fgsm_test) The cleverhans test script is just a shortened version of one of their [tutorials](https://github.com/tensorflow/cleverhans/blob/master/cleverhans_tutorials/mnist_tutorial_keras_tf.py) without the adversarial training. ,

issue_title: Example doesn't seem to work
issue_body:
Hi,
I tried following the instructions mentioned in the [tutorial](http://foolbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/tutorial.html)and [examples ](http://foolbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/examples.html#applying-an-attack) sections, and create an attack for a VGG19 model. Downloaded the model's checkpoint from [here](https://github.com/tensorflow/models/tree/master/slim)

The code "runs" however it doesn't seem to be able to generate an adversarial example - the process never ends... What am I doing wrong? 

Please advise

```

import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.contrib.slim.nets import vgg
import numpy as np
import foolbox
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from foolbox.attacks import LBFGSAttack
from foolbox.criteria import TargetClassProbability

images = tf.placeholder(tf.float32, shape=(None, 224, 224, 3))
preprocessed = images - [123.68, 116.78, 103.94]
logits, _ = vgg.vgg_19(images, is_training=False)
restorer = tf.train.Saver(tf.trainable_variables())

image, _ = foolbox.utils.imagenet_example()

with foolbox.models.TensorFlowModel(images, logits, (0, 255)) as model:
    restorer.restore(model.session, "./vgg_19.ckpt")
    print(np.argmax(model.predictions(image)))
    target_class = 22
    criterion = TargetClassProbability(target_class, p=0.01)

    attack = LBFGSAttack(model, criterion)
    label = np.argmax(model.predictions(image))

    adversarial = attack(image=image, label=label)

    plt.subplot(1, 3, 1)
    plt.imshow(image)

    plt.subplot(1, 3, 2)
    plt.imshow(adversarial)

    plt.subplot(1, 3, 3)
    plt.imshow(adversarial - image)
```

issue_title: Handling sub-models with unknown fields
issue_body:
I'm trying to express a schema where a sub-object is allowed to contain extra (unknown) fields. The only way to skip unknown fields I can find is using `strict=False` argument when importing/validating data, allowing them anywhere in the schema.
Is it possible to add some more fine-grained control? It seems that there was a `ModelType(A, strict=False)` parameter previously, but it was removed in #359.
Allowing unknown fields sometimes is a property of a data type, not a single import/validation operation.

issue_title: For Share/Deliver, register users in DukeDS as needed
issue_body:
The API to create a DukeDS user is at https://apidev.dataservice.duke.edu/apidocs/users_software_agents.html#auth-provider-affiliates-not_impl_new-create-or-get-user-account-for-affiliate-post, and while we can register users through this API, DukeDS will not automatically create new accounts until we call it.

Since the [share/deliver actions in DukeDSClient](https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSClient/blob/master/ddsc/ddsclient.py#L260) already have a step to look up the user by email or netid, this would be a good time to register the user account if needed.

I considered adding this step to D4S2, but D4S2 currently only deals with the UUIDs of existing users. It assumes the user has already been looked up.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Lint does not notice missing tool_data_table_conf.xml.sample
Command to convert tool_dependencies.xml recipes into shell scripts
issue_body:
Related to #19 (testing ``tool_dependencies.xml`` without a tool shed), I would like to be able to run an install recipe from a ``tool_dependencies.xml`` file locally and/or turn it into a simple shell script for the current platform.

(The platform specific actions could be turned into bash if statements if preferred)

This seems to overlap with https://github.com/jmchilton/shed2tap

e.g. https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/blob/master/tools/effectiveT3/tool_dependencies.xml

```
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<tool_dependency>
    <package name="effectiveT3" version="1.0.1">
        <install version="1.0">
            <actions>
                <!-- Set environment variable so Python script knows where to look -->
                <action type="set_environment">
                    <environment_variable name="EFFECTIVET3" action="set_to">$INSTALL_DIR</environment_variable>
                </action>
                <!-- Main JAR file -->
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_GUI-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <!-- If using action type download_file will need to move the file,
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_GUI-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/</destination></action>
                -->
                <!-- Three model JAR files -->
                <action type="make_directory">$INSTALL_DIR/module</action>
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>        
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
            </actions>
        </install>
        <readme>
Downloads effectiveT3 v1.0.1 and the three models from http://effectors.org/ aka http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/
        </readme>
    </package>
</tool_dependency>
```

Would become something like this (assuming already in install directory as per XML convention):

```
#!/bin/bash
#House keeping: strict bash mode, etc
set -euo pipefail
export INSTALL_DIR=$PWD
#Start of conversion from XML recipe:
echo "Installing effectiveT3 version 1.0.1"
export EFFECTIVET3=$INSTALL_DIR
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_GUI-1.0.1.jar
mkdir $INSTALL_DIR/module
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
```

I would then be able to run this within TravisCI with the advantages that the install recipe is not repeated (``tool_dependencies.xml`` and ``.travis.yml``) and moreover I would actually be able to test ``tool_dependencies.xml``, e.g. https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/commit/243311cc50ab2675c5e6aa42524841e60e6602e8

issue_title: Generalized Test Follow Up
issue_body:
A list of tasks to enhance the newer planemo capability to test Galaxy workflows, cwl tools (with Galaxy or cwltool), and cwl workflows (with cwltool). New testing was added in #454  and scoped out in #448.

 - [ ] Document the ``<artifact>-test.yml`` format.
 - [ ] Refactor much of Galaxy's testing comparison stuff out into galaxy-lib or a new testing package for reuse in planemo.
 - [ ] Develop a test syntax to leverage above library from ``<artifact>-test.yml`` tests.
 - [ ] Implement XUnit reports for newer testing stuff (html, markdown, json, and text already work).
 - [ ] Extend CWL ``tool_init`` to build a test case.

issue_title: Figure out what to do about samtools dependency on upload.
issue_body: There should be a ``--skip_upload_dependencies`` option and if it is installed once it should always be available.

issue_title: --no-cache should probably use a temporary cache instead of nothing
issue_body: `peru sync --no-cache` usually winds up importing the same module more than once, because everywhere we ask for its tree we get a cache miss and redo it. We will probably want to switch to a temporary cache, or maybe an in-memory cache, in order to avoid this.

issue_title: External per-device keychains
issue_body:
@shea256 points out that we not only need per-device keychains (one for your laptop, one for your phone, etc.), but also that these keychains must be external to Blockstack.  This is because your phone can't run a Core node, or have any long-running process.

What this means for Core is that there's some more work to be done on the storage end of things:

* A name's zone files should have the signing public key *for each device* in its zone file.  For example, my zone file might have something like this:

```
$ORIGIN judecn.id
$TTL 3600
pubkey.19db6d10-5cac-43d3-a055-b11e9d0e6cc7 TXT "pubkey:data:03cabba0b5b9a871dbaa11c044066e281c5feb57243c7d2a452f06a0d708613a46"
pubkey.c6b22bea-379c-424a-925c-9d3aad465128 TXT "pubkey:data:0267cd02e6ceae7ed58e3c8ca856633bf5424ccce73c44f0eb7b71d1fe3a6f5e0f"
```

where my device-specific public keys are named as `pubkey.{uuid4}`.

* Devices need a way to share their datastore-specific public keys.  This is problematic, because datastore private keys are hardened children of the device's signing private key---one device cannot derive the datastore public key of another device.  The device that wishes to share must somehow broadcast an attestation from its signing key that the datastore public key is specific to the given application.
    * Maybe datastore public keys can go into the name's profile?
    * Maybe a datastore private key can be an unhardened child of the signing key, but with 256 bits of entropy to make a brute-force search intractable?

* The RESTful API authentication endpoint needs to take a public key from the application, and not rely on the device-specific private key.
    * Blockstack libraries will need to generate the signatures themselves.
    * We'll need to keep the storage-write interface simple: just a signature and the raw payload.

* Various refactoring will need to be done internally to support multiple data public keys.

issue_title: Collect logs for debugging 8 node cluster failure
Option to Error with Default on New Field
issue_body:
When adding a column to an existing table with a default value, django will lock the table while performing the update. Django best practices are to create a new column with `null=True` and *no* default value. 

Reference: 

[Stack Overflow Summary](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/41502331/migration-of-django-field-with-default-value-to-postgresql-database)

[Django Docs](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/migrations/#backend-support)

Can you add an option to lint for this best practice. Deploying to a production server with a default value set has a high probability of resulting in downtime.

As in the title, this issue is created for discussion about the consistency in requirement of 'partition' parameter across the SDK, it seems that in majority of  cases we do not need 'partition' to create resource, however it would make sense to have this globally enforced when creating new resources from now, as a good practice.

Thoughts?

issue_title: ProductSelect widget has css class(es) overriden by widget tweaks render_field template tag
issue_body:
ProductSelect widgets require the `select2` css class in order for the widget to be useable in the dashboard.

In the template `partials/form_field.html` the `render_field` tag is trying to amend the css class `form-control` but ends up overriding the css classes set via the ProductSelect widget class. Without the select2 class the widgets are not initialised properly and therefore not useable in the dashboard.

I'm working on a PR for this issue now.

issue_title: Extract flatpages feature in separate package
issue_body:
Providing the flatpages features makes it more difficult to integrate Oscar in a CMS since it causes a duplication of features and forces to enable a potentially useless app for Oscar to work (flatpages), as well as to hack around to hide this feature from the dashboard.

I think it would be better to provide this feature as a separate package.

issue_title: How do you dependency inject the ILoggerFactory in .net core 2.x
issue_body:
Problem:

With asp.net core 1.x, you are able to dependency inject the ILoggerFactory into the controller via Startup::Configure:

```
public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app,
    IHostingEnvironment env,
    ILoggerFactory loggerFactory)
```

However, with 2.x the ILoggerFactory is no longer passed as a param to Configure.  It's not clear to me how you would DI the ILoggerFactory into your classes without this param.

Thanks,
Derek

issue_body:
# **Remote debugger does not work - arm64v8 .NET Core 2.1 C# console app**

# General
We should be able to run and debug .**NET Core 2.1 C# console app on arm64v8 within a Docker container**.
**DragonBoard 820c** is a powerful single board computer to be used as an **Azure IoT Edge** hub.
As many other developers, I'd like to use the arm64v8 **DragonBoard 820c** to develop powerful **IoT** and **IoMT** apps.
Let's hope that this will be achieved by .NET Core 2.1 RTM!

**Run**
I can only run .NET Core 2.0 in arm64v8 Docker if I use the source below:
`Microsoft/dotnet:2.0.5-runtime-stretch-arm32v7`
I can't run .NET Core 2.1 at all!

**Debug**
`./getvsdbgshbeta -v latest -r linux-arm -l ~/vsdbg?` does not work for .NET Core 2.0.
It's from https://vsdebugger.azureedge.net/vsdbg-15-1-11120-1/vsdbg-linux-arm.zip.
I can't debug .NET Core 2.0 nor .NET Core 2.1 at all!

issue_title: Product Version appears to not get set in SCD build while it does for FDD build
issue_body:
# Issue Title

Product Version appears to not get set in SCD build while it does for FDD build

# General

.net core 2.0, Windows 7 64 bit

dotnet build 

will produce a dll with the correct project version in the Product Version attribute of the .dll file.  However when we do a SCD build as in 

dotnet publish -f netcoreapp2.0 -c Release -r win-x64

The dotnet.exe that has been renamed to the project at the end of the publish command is missing the Product Version that matches the project, and other expected fields, like Title, Company, Authors, etc.

issue_title: Question: Is .Net Core written ALL from scratch?
issue_body:
Hi,

Sorry if this place and question is inappropriate then please close the issue and my apologies.

But, if I am allowed to ask then could some one actually let me understand the history and the development of .Net core which I have been wondering
- Is the .Net Core written all from scratch?
- If not then are there still some legacy code? 
- How was the legacy code refactored to be made open source?
- How is the testing done for legacy code here? I see C# code is using NUnit so how about the legacy code?

issue_title: Have you broken Kestrel configuration?
issue_body:
# Issue Title

Have you broken Kestrel configuration?

# General

In .Net Core 1.0 in Main you used to be able to '.Build()' on the builder to get the configuration resolved, in order to be able to use configuration in .UseKestrel(...)

Unless I am mistaken, that is now not possible in 2.1 - one can only use constants or read from files by hand.
Am I mistaken?

cheers

Matt

On the dialog screen when there is an issue with a user's email, the text "Enter email address (used for urgent notices and lost key recovery)" is printed as the end of a paragraph with the next line not being a input area but another paragraph of text. It makes the screen a little awkward to read and unclear.

The text looks like this:

issue_title: Mac OS X Server "/.well-known/acme-challenge/***KEY***"
issue_body:
I know that this is how letsencrypt verifies that I own the server, however when I make the required file for this URL and navigate to it this is what OS X Server produces:

> Websites are turned off.
> An administrator can turn them on using the Server application.

Does anybody know how I can fix this issue?

issue_title: cannot run letsencrypt as a systemd service
issue_body:
I hacked up an autorenewal script that is supposed to run as a systemd service, activated by a timer unit once per week. Here's the code for the script and the systemd service: https://github.com/majewsky/system-configuration/blob/4fcde370974fcfb2a595f7f81297b807b895e002/hologram-nginx-and-letsencrypt.pkg.toml#L153-L198

The script runs fine on my system when invoked as `sudo -u letsencrypt -g letsencrypt /usr/bin/letsencrypt-allofthem`, but fails with the following trace when run via `systemctl start letsencrypt-allofthem.service`:

```
2016-02-22 17:05:38,984:DEBUG:letsencrypt.cli:Root logging level set at 30
2016-02-22 17:05:38,990:INFO:letsencrypt.cli:Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
2016-02-22 17:05:38,995:DEBUG:letsencrypt.cli:letsencrypt version: 0.4.0
2016-02-22 17:05:38,996:DEBUG:letsencrypt.cli:Arguments: ['--agree-tos', '--keep-until-expiring', '--webroot', '-w', '/srv/letsencrypt/', '-d', 'repo.holocm.org']
2016-02-22 17:05:38,999:DEBUG:letsencrypt.cli:Discovered plugins: PluginsRegistry(PluginEntryPoint#webroot,PluginEntryPoint#null,PluginEntryPoint#manual,PluginEntryPoint#standalone)
2016-02-22 17:05:39,036:DEBUG:letsencrypt.cli:Exiting abnormally:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/sbin/letsencrypt", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('letsencrypt==0.4.0', 'console_scripts', 'letsencrypt')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/letsencrypt/cli.py", line 1978, in main
    "Root (sudo) is required to run most of letsencrypt functionality.")
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 1171, in warning
    self._log(WARNING, msg, args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 1278, in _log
    self.handle(record)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 1288, in handle
    self.callHandlers(record)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 1328, in callHandlers
    hdlr.handle(record)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 751, in handle
    self.emit(record)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/letsencrypt/log.py", line 64, in emit
    self.width + self.PADDING_WIDTH)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dialog.py", line 2675, in infobox
    kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dialog.py", line 1765, in _widget_with_no_output
    widget_name, output))
PythonDialogBug
```

It appears to be crashing when trying to print a "you must be root" warning, because its stdin is connected to `/dev/null`. Because it's the "you must be root" warning, I want to note explicitly that runnign as non-root is **not** the problem. When invoked as `sudo -u letsencrypt -g letsencrypt /usr/bin/letsencrypt-allofthem` from an interactive session, the script completes successfully (although printing a useless "you must be root" warning).

I tried to work around the problem by specifying `StandardInput=tty` in the systemd service file, but that just causes `systemctl start` to hang, with `ps aux` showing `systemd-tty-ask-password-agent` and `pkttyagent` processes that I don't know what they do:

```
root     21255  1.0  0.0  71576  5240 pts/0    S+   18:21   0:00 sudo systemctl start --job-mode=ignore-dependencies letsencrypt-allofthem.service
root     21256  0.0  0.0  21556  2504 pts/0    S+   18:21   0:00 systemctl start --job-mode=ignore-dependencies letsencrypt-allofthem.service
root     21257  0.0  0.0   8488  1516 pts/0    S+   18:21   0:00 /usr/bin/systemd-tty-ask-password-agent --watch
root     21258  0.0  0.0 273400  4244 pts/0    Sl+  18:21   0:00 /usr/bin/pkttyagent --notify-fd 4 --fallback
stefan   21262  0.0  0.0  35872  3340 pts/1    R+   18:21   0:00 ps aux
```

Any ideas how to workaround this? I'm tempted to just comment out the "you must be root" warning in the source code, but that will certainly break with the next update of the letsencrypt package. A proper solution might be handling non-interactive sessions without a useful FD 0 with more care.

The involved software versions are systemd-229 and letsencrypt-0.4.0, both installed from the standard system packages from my distribution:

```
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Arch Linux"
ID=arch
PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
ANSI_COLOR="0;36"
HOME_URL="https://www.archlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://bbs.archlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.archlinux.org/"
```

issue_title: obtaining certs taking an unusual amount of time
issue_body: On my VPS installing certs take roughly ~40 minutes with https reconnecting the whole time. It's almost as if i've being rate limited to 1byte/s by Boulder. There's no firewall running on my VPS.

issue_title: Stop supporting legacy Python versions

The plan is to limit compatibility of the following:.

    python-2 < 2.7
    python-3 < 3.3

One purpose is to demotivate the use of really old python releases.
Another is to allow using newer syntax when programming it.

For older python-2 version, use version `1.1.x` series.

issue_title: No way of generating recurrences backwards?
issue_body:
I can generate recurrences forwards…

```
future = datetime.now()
while True:
    future = recurrences.after(future, inc=True)
    if future is None:
        return
    yield future
```

…but AFAIU `rrule.before()` can't generate recurrences backwards, it needs an initial date and it will generate recurrences from that date onwards. Does dateutil provide some way of doing this? If not, could something similar be added?

The only workaround I can see at this stage is to generate recurrences for some time span, feed them into a `queue.PriorityQueue` where they get ordered and yield them from there.

issue_title: No returns the sent message
Multiple clients with same credentials
issue_body:
<!--
0. The library is Python 3.x, not Python 2.x.
1. If you're posting an issue, make sure it's a bug in the library, not in your code.
2. If you're posting a question, make sure you have read and tried enough things first.
3. Show as much information as possible, including your failed attempts, and the full console output (to include the whole traceback with line numbers).
4. Good looking issues are a lot more appealing. If you need help check out https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/.

You may also want to watch "How (not) to ask a technical question" over https://youtu.be/53zkBvL4ZB4
-->

Hello.

First of all, **thanks** for such a nice library.

I have a **question** about creating multiple instances of `client` with the **same** credentials.

### The story
Let's say, i have a `main.py`, when i do all the required stuff to make it work and then start `idle`.

But one day, i wanted to send a message asynchronously using `Celery`. Since i **can't** pass `client` as an argument to the task, i have to **create** a **new** **instance** of `client` and send a message. And it **worked**! Message from the _Celery task_ was successfully sent. **But...** 

Remember `main.py` and its client that is in `idle` "mode"? It **does not see** any new updates. It is still `is_connected==True` and `is_user_authorized==True`, even more, it is still trying hard to work: 
```
DEBUG:telethon.telegram_bare_client:Receiving items from the network timed out
DEBUG:telethon.telegram_bare_client:Receiving items from the network...
DEBUG:telethon.extensions.tcp_client:socket.timeout "timed out" while reading data
DEBUG:telethon.telegram_bare_client:Receiving items from the network timed out
...
and so on
```

### Trying to fight
I was investigating this problem for awhile. 

**What i have tried**:
- to set `update_workers` to `1` in the`main.py`, in the `task` and in both
- to play with `spawn_read_thread`
- to manually disconnect client inside the `task` after the job was done

Nothing of listed above helped. :( 

### THE WIN!
But apparently, i have found something interesting in the _docs_ - `.sync_updates()`. Well, i decided to run this every time task was done. But how should i do this? `idle` locked my `main.py`, so i had to get rid of it. So, i wrote my own simple
```python
while True:
    sleep(1)
    client.sync_updates()
```

And it **worked**!

It seems like, when i called `connect` in another `client` "entity", it acquired update handling, so my `main.py` `client` couldn't  receive anything.

### Questions
 - Is this a proper way of handling this kind of problem or you can suggest my anything else?
 - Is `.sync_updates()` cheap or expensive? I am a little worried how calling this every 1 second will effect performance? 
- If it is a good way of handling such problems and it is not expensive, is it possible to add this thing to `idle`? :-)

Regards,
@Senpos

issue_title: client.idle() and set_update_state

issue_title: IFrame support on Windows
issue_body:
IE and Edge does not support IFrame with data uri as src attribute. Instead both support only explicit paths for src attribute. 

So folumn IFrames does not show on Windows browsers.

issue_title: map.geo_json geo_path parameter does not work on relative paths
issue_body:
Say if I had the following from the cloropleth examples:
```python
import folium
import pandas as pd

state_geo = r'us-states.json'
state_unemployment = r'US_Unemployment_Oct2012.csv'

state_data = pd.read_csv(state_unemployment)

#Let Folium determine the scale
map = folium.Map(location=[48, -102], zoom_start=3)
map.geo_json(geo_path=state_geo, data=state_data,
             columns=['State', 'Unemployment'],
             key_on='feature.id',
             fill_color='YlGn', fill_opacity=0.7, line_opacity=0.2,
             legend_name='Unemployment Rate (%)')
map.create_map(path='us_states.html')
```

it would work fine. But if my state_geo is something like ```r'../data/us-states.json``` it wont work at all nor through any errors only a java script one when you open the html in the browser. This seems to be a bug worth looking in to.

issue_title: Heatmaps on Folium
issue_body: Is there a way to use heatmaps on folium w/o going back to Leaflet.js and its heatmap plugin set?

issue_body:
When rate limited by Conversation, the error thrown by the SDK is vague:
```

cURL reveals the actual service response to be this (as plain text):

issue_title: Move Marshaller and Unmarshaller out of `fields` module
issue_body: `Marshaller` and `Unmarshaller` are considered to be private API whereas the rest of the classes in `marshmallow.fields` is public API. Therefore, it makes sense to separate those two classes into their own module.

issue_title: Unset value on ToOneField makes it None during hydration
issue_body:
If there's a foreign key on a resource, and the bundle data doesn't include that field, the foreign key field gets set to None during the hydration process, even if it already has a value.

The following test case illustrates this (and also how it behaves differently from a non-relational field)

``` python
def test_full_hydrate__does_not_overwrite_related_value_if_not_put(self):
        class RelatedBasicResource(BasicResource):
            parent = fields.ToOneField(BasicResource, 'parent', null=True, blank=True)
        basic = RelatedBasicResource()
        basic_bundle_1 = Bundle(data={
            'name': 'Daniel',
            'date_joined': None,
        })
        basic_bundle_1.obj = Mock()
        basic_bundle_1.obj.date_joined = aware_datetime(2010, 2, 15, 12, 0, 0)
        basic_bundle_1.obj.view_count = 3
        basic_bundle_1.obj.parent = Mock()

        self.assertNotEqual(basic_bundle_1.obj.view_count, None)
        self.assertNotEqual(basic_bundle_1.obj.parent, None)

        # Now load up the data.
        hydrated = basic.full_hydrate(basic_bundle_1)

        self.assertNotEqual(hydrated.obj.view_count, None)
        self.assertNotEqual(hydrated.obj.parent, None)
```

I believe https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/commit/73425c31ce732646c6b2299feba4b913d9e93387 is the cause of this. I see the problem in 0.13.3 and the latest repo version but not in 0.13.2.

The field is both blank and null but it now hits the null case before the blank case and therefore gets set to None rather than falling through. I don't know tastypie well enough to have the right solution but hopefully this information can shed some light for somebody :)

issue_title: Check size when adding module to container
issue_body:
If user put the following two modules together
```scala
Sequential[Float]()
    .add(Linear(3, 4))
    .add(Linear(3, 4))
```
Although the sizes do not match, the exception will be thrown till runtime. It's better if there's a way to report errors when defining the model.

Each module may need to tell how to map an input size to output. The container may have an input shape, and if it's set, when some modules are added to the container, the shape will check.

```scala
Sequential[Float]().setInputShape([-1, 3])  // -1 means batch
    .add(Linear(3, 4))
    .add(Linear(3, 4))  // throw an shape mismatch error
```

issue_title: pvlib python communication forums
issue_body:
What do people think about these new communication forums?

- pvlib tag on stackoverflow: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pvlib
- pvlib python google group: https://groups.google.com/forum/m/#!forum/pvlib-python
- pvlib python slack organization: https://pvlib-python.slack.com/

Here are my thoughts:

First, all credit goes to @mikofski for pushing this forward and setting up some things for us to try! Thanks!

I am concerned that too many forums could be counter productive, but I think we're big enough now that we could benefit from more than GitHub issues. Watching the project on GitHub does allow people to stay up to date on every little thing in the pvlib python world, but that is way too much information for many people. Spreading the communication over multiple platforms lets people focus on what they really care about at the cost of making things more complicated and potentially diluting the viewership of any given item.

I think it would be nice if we would use GitHub for bug tracking and development discussions, people would ask usage questions on Stack Overflow, and we’d use a mailing list for new version announcements. 

I know that a few people have tried to ask pvlib questions on stack overflow in the past with varying degrees of success. I am going to start watching Mark's new pvlib tag. I don't have much experience with google groups, but at this point I prefer stackoverflow for usage questions. In any case, while we're getting started with stack overflow and/or google groups, I think that people should feel free to open and then close a GitHub issue that links to the new post.

On a related note, [Sibbell](https://sibbell.com/about/) is a nice tool that will send you an email when there are new releases of any of your starred repositories. 

I like Slack for quick, informal conversations in which I don’t have to stress about if I’m going to come across the wrong way in a public forum. I don't like the constant din of interruptions on Slack, though.

I also get requests for help via email from time to time. I don't like answering questions via email because only a handful of people have an opportunity to learn from the exchange.

I don’t know what the ideal approach is, but I think we should try something new and see if it catches on!

Finally, we should add some notes to the documentation once we come to a consensus.

issue_title: fix linke turbidity time interpolation
issue_body:
As indicated in [in `clearsky.lookup_linke_turbidity` on L212-224](../blob/master/pvlib/clearsky.py#L212-L224)

> This is approximate and could be made more accurate.

```python
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.linspace(-15, 380, num=14)
array([ -15.        ,   15.38461538,   45.76923077,   76.15384615,
        106.53846154,  136.92307692,  167.30769231,  197.69230769,
        228.07692308,  258.46153846,  288.84615385,  319.23076923,
        349.61538462,  380.        ])
```

Another method would be to use the calendar month days:

```python
>>> import calendar
>>> import numpy as np
>>> mdays = calendar.mdays[1:]
>>> np.concatenate([[-calendar.mdays[1] / 2.0],
                    np.cumsum(mdays) - np.array(mdays) / 2.,
                    [365 + calendar.mdays[-1] / 2.0]])
array([ -15.5,   15.5,   45. ,   74.5,  105. ,  135.5,  166. ,  196.5,
        227.5,  258. ,  288.5,  319. ,  349.5,  380.5])
```

This still doesn't account for leap years, but we can use `calendar.isleap(<year>)`:
```python
import calendar
import numpy as np


def calendar_month_middles(year):
    mdays = np.array(calendar.mdays[1:])  # copy
    if calendar.isleap(year):
        mdays[1] = mdays[1] + 1
    return np.concatenate([[-calendar.mdays[1] / 2.0],
                    np.cumsum(mdays) - np.array(mdays) / 2.,
                    [365 + calendar.mdays[-1] / 2.0]])
```

now you can see the difference in leap year

```python
>>> calendar_month_middles(2016)  # a leap year
array([ -15.5,   15.5,   45.5,   75.5,  106. ,  136.5,  167. ,  197.5,
        228.5,  259. ,  289.5,  320. ,  350.5,  380.5])
>>> calendar_month_middles(2015)  # NOT a leap year
array([ -15.5,   15.5,   45. ,   74.5,  105. ,  135.5,  166. ,  196.5,
        227.5,  258. ,  288.5,  319. ,  349.5,  380.5])
```

issue_title: Suggestion: short-circuit `and` and `or` operators when of the operators is a constant
issue_body:
Hi,

I suggest adding the following trivial transformation when creating disjunctions and conjunctions when one argument is a boolean constant:

- `and(x, true) -> x`
- `and(x, false) -> false`
- `or(x, true) -> true`
- `or(x, false) -> x`

Many other solvers do it already, and I think such a transformation is:

- very cheap.
- allows for nicer introspection: currently without (sometimes very expensive) simplification there is no easy way to check that the formula `and(true, true, true, true)` is just `true`.
- creates more human-readable formulas in the end, omitting redundant information.

I can try to write a pull request if this is a good idea.

issue_title: SMTLib compliance: quotes for echo
issue_body:
Page 67 of http://smtlib.cs.uiowa.edu/papers/smt-lib-reference-v2.6-draft-3.pdf says:

> `(echo s)` where s is a string literal, simply prints back s as is—including the surrounding
> double-quotes.

Strictly speaking, z3 is violating this as it does not print the surrounding quotes. I think the motivation is that the output of all commands are valid s-expressions, and putting the quotes would ensure this invariant is always held.

Perhaps not the most important detail, but would be good to be compliant in this case as well.

issue_title: Ensure objects are registered when assigning them to other objects
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Oliver Ruebel (Bitbucket: [oruebel](https://bitbucket.org/oruebel), GitHub: [oruebel](https://github.com/oruebel))**

----------------------------------------

The problem this ticket describes is the following. In serveral cases, NWBContainers are closely tied to the parent container E.g, SpikeUnit to UnitTimes, Device to NWBFile etc. In these cases, the API often provides dedicated methods on the main type to create these contained types, however, if the user creates the contained objects independently using the corresponding class then we need to make sure that the parent gets set correctly to ensure the objects are written correctly. For example: 

* The function NWBFile.create_device works but when creating a Device directly and setting it only on the ElectrodeGroup then the Device may not be registered with the NWBFile so that the link for device in ElectrodeGroup will be borken because the device is not written. To avoid this we need to register the Device with the NWBFile as well. 
* UnitTimes requires a list of SpikeUnit as input. However, the user needs to make sure that both the SpikeUnit are assigned to UnitTimes as well as that the parent on SpikeUnit is set correctly in order for the SpikeUnit. 


```
#!python

    spike_unit_objs = [SpikeUnit(....) for s in spike_unit_data]
    unit_times = UnitTimes(source="Data as reported in the original crcns file",
                           spike_units=spike_unit_objs)
    for i in spike_unit_objs:   # We now need to set the parent for the spike_unit_obj
        i.parent = unit_times
```

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lblneuro/pynwb/issue/43

issue_title: sim_proc for strcpy on tainted source seems not to finish
issue_body:
I have a very simple binary (available [here](https://www.ctrlc.hu/~stef/assignment2)) which calls strcpy with argv[1] as source, and dest is a fixed size buffer on the stack. additionally there is a function you_win in the binary, which gets called if i pass ```'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa\x14\x84\x04\x08'``` or equivalent as argv[1].

The following script returns after 3 seconds if i disable sim_procedures with a result of 100 bytes of zero (which makes little sense in this case) and if i enable sim_procedures the script never finishes and keeps step()-ing: 
```
#!/usr/bin/env python

import os
import sys
import angr
from simuvex import s_options as so
import IPython
import logging

l = logging.getLogger("angr")
l.setLevel("INFO")

def fully_symbolic(state, variable):
    '''
    check if a symbolic variable is completely symbolic
    '''
    for i in range(state.arch.bits):
        if not state.se.symbolic(variable[i]):
            return False

    return True

def main(binary):
    binary_name = os.path.basename(binary)

    p = angr.Project(binary, use_sim_procedures=True, load_options={'auto_load_libs':True})

    # argv1 is symbolic
    argv1 = angr.claripy.BVS("argv1",100*8) #since we do not the length now, we just put 100 bytes

    extras = {so.REVERSE_MEMORY_NAME_MAP, so.TRACK_ACTION_HISTORY, so.UNICORN}
    es = p.factory.entry_state(args=[binary,argv1], add_options=extras) #, remove_options={so.LAZY_SOLVES})
    pg = p.factory.path_group(es, veritesting=False, save_unconstrained=True)

    # find a bug giving us control of PC
    l.info("looking for vulnerability in '%s'", binary_name)

    exploitable_path = None
    while exploitable_path is None:
        r = pg.step()
        if r.errored:
            print r.errored
            sys.exit(1)
        if len(pg.unconstrained) > 0:
            l.info("found some unconstrained paths, checking exploitability")
            for u in pg.unconstrained:
                if fully_symbolic(u.state, u.state.regs.pc):
                    exploitable_path = u
                    break

            # no exploitable path found, drop them
            pg.drop(stash='unconstrained')

    if not exploitable_path:
        l.info("no exploitable path found")
        sys.exit(1)

    l.info("found a path which looks exploitable")
    ep = exploitable_path

    assert ep.state.se.symbolic(ep.state.regs.pc), "PC must be symbolic at this point"

    l.info("attempting to create exploit based on found path")
    target=p.loader.main_bin.get_symbol("you_win").addr

    if not ep.state.satisfiable(extra_constraints=[ep.state.regs.pc == target]):
        l.info("cannot point pc to you_win")
        sys.exit(1)

    l.info("pointing pc towards you_win 0x%08x" % target)
    ep.state.add_constraints(ep.state.regs.pc == target)

    l.info("solution: `./%s %s`" % (binary_name, repr(ep.state.se.any_str(argv1))))
    IPython.embed()

    return 0

if __name__ == '__main__':
    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1]))
    else:
        print "%s: <binary>" % sys.argv[0]
```

What is wrong with my approach or the simuvex implementation of strcpy?

issue_title: CMClient: Refactor reconnection code
issue_body:
It's best to leave reconnection logic to the application, however there are certain cases where it seems better to automatically reconnect/retry.

* Trying to connect to CM, but timing out during `connect()`

Currently considering removing automatic reconnect, except for while trying connect(), and providing a reconnect method that will do exponential backoff. This way the application can then just do:

```python
client.on('disconnect', client.reconnect)

reconnect will essentially be connect with delay. The delay will be reset on successful login.

issue_title: Vehicle.commands.goto always treats alt as relative
issue_body:
Vehicle.commands.goto takes a LocationGlobal or a LocationGlobalRelative but either way it assumes the alt value is relative. This means that if you pass it a LocationGlobal and say "goto" it will fly to that position ABOVE the home position (rather than mean sea level).

issue_title: Nonblocking option isn't available for MacOS because pcap.h file is parsed incorrectly
issue_body:
The `pypcap` library doesn't support nonblocking mode when fully built on MacOS version 10.13.4.

I see how `pcap.h` source files / dependencies are found via `setup.py`:
```
def get_extension():
    # A list of all the possible search directories
    dirs = ['/usr', sys.prefix] + glob.glob('/opt/libpcap*') + \
        glob.glob('../libpcap*') + glob.glob('../wpdpack*') + \
        glob.glob('/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/' +
                  'MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/*')

    for d in dirs:
        search_dirs = [
            os.path.join(d, 'local', 'include'),
            os.path.join(d, 'usr', 'include'),
            os.path.join(d, 'include'),
            d
        ]

        pcap_h = recursive_search_dirs(search_dirs, ['pcap.h'])
        if pcap_h:
            print("Found pcap headers in %s" % pcap_h)
            break

    if not pcap_h:
        print("pcap.h not found")
        sys.exit(1)

    include_dirs = [os.path.dirname(pcap_h)]
```
My `/usr/include/pcap.h` has this:
`#include <pcap/pcap.h>`

while my `/usr/include/pcap/pcap.h` has this:
```
PCAP_API int    pcap_setdirection(pcap_t *, pcap_direction_t);                  
PCAP_API int    pcap_getnonblock(pcap_t *, char *);                                                                                                                       
PCAP_API int    pcap_setnonblock(pcap_t *, int, char *);                        
PCAP_API int    pcap_inject(pcap_t *, const void *, size_t);
```
Unfortunately, I believe you're looking for the function signatures in the wrong file (`/usr/include/pcap.h`), and that file in turn includes a separate header with the function signatures you're looking for.

issue_title: Move to IPython 3.x by default
issue_body:
I would like to see us move to IPython 3.x by default by 6.1.13.

I think this is as working as well as the presently default IPython 0.6 implementation we're using.

It would be nice to drop the old IPython 0.6 related code once we've settled on IPython 3 as it's unlikely we will have anyone dropping back to an older version any time soon.

I would push for it for 6.1.12 but there have been a lot of changes in Core which I'd like to see tested and released first and whilst IPython shouldn't interfere with this I'd rather not risk too many things changing under the hood in 6.1.12.

Does anyone have a strong objection to this move?

Thanks,

Rob

issue_title: Trigger events only when transaction is committed
issue_body:
Currently notifications are triggered when the resource method is executed.

For batch requests, if one of the sub-requests fails, the whole batched is rolledback. However the events are still triggered.

The events should be triggered when the batch transaction is committed.

This will also affect performance since only one event per collection will be triggered for a batch request.

issue_title: Refactor SQLQuery, SparkStorecommand and Command
issue_body:
The SparkStoreCommand class introduced in #333 looks a lot like SQLQuery class. The straight-forward action is to refactor so that the new class and SQLQuery inherit from some hybrid.

However. if we include Command, they are all 'commands' in the general sense, and all (directly or indirectly) inherit from ObjectWithGuid. Due to that, perhaps we should have SQLQuery, SparkStoreCommand and future types of commands to inherit from Command.

issue_title: WebUI logging strategy
issue_body:
The WebUI uses a Python logger to make its log. furthermore, the uWSGI also makes its own log.

As a result,we may have several log files:

 - [ ] webui-access.log, from uWSGI, that will contain the uWSGI HTTP requests
 - [ ] webui-error.log, from uWSGI, that will contain the WebUI console log
 - [ ] info.log, from WebUI, that will contain the INFO level log
 - [ ] warning.log, from WebUI, that will contain the WARNING level log
 - [ ] error.log, from WebUI, that will contain the ERROR level log

After some experiments, the 3 last configured files are not really interesting. Only both first files are pertinent. As such, I will make a default configuration with only the uWSGI log files activated.

Changing the application log level is done thanks to the `logging.json` file or an environment variable to set the application DEBUG mode.

issue_title: POTCAR modifications ignored in elastic workflow
issue_body:
## System

* Version: master
* Python version: 3.5.3
* OS version: fedora 25

## Summary

* Modifying the `vasp_input_set` as recommended [here](https://hackingmaterials.github.io/atomate/customizing_workflows.html#use-a-different-potcar) is ignored after the workflow is generated.
* `PAW_PBE Sm_3 07Sep2000` is used by default, I would like to use `PAW_PBE Sm 21Nov2014`

## Example code

```python
from pymatgen.io.vasp.inputs import Poscar
from pymatgen.io.vasp.sets import MPStaticSet
from atomate.vasp.workflows.base.elastic import get_wf_elastic_constant
from fireworks import LaunchPad

lp = LaunchPad.auto_load()
poscar = Poscar.from_file("POSCAR")
structure = poscar.structure
vis_input = MPStaticSet(structure)
vis_input.config_dict['POTCAR']['Sm'] = 'Sm'   # Actually changed here
wf = get_wf_elastic_constant(structure, vasp_input_set=vis_input)
lp.add_wf(wf)
```

## Error message

* N/A

## Suggested solution (if any)

* It seems to be passed correctly all the way to TransmuterFW. I'm not sure what causes it to be modified later. 

* If no solution is easily available, is there an equivalent to add_modify_incar (but add_modify_potcar or similar) that can work as a post-fix?

https://github.com/pulp/pulp/pull/2460

Unfortunately, this happens:

```
$ time psmash
~ ~
flake8 . --ignore D203
pylint -j 4 --reports=n --ignore-imports=y --disable=I \
        docs/conf.py \
        setup.py \
        tests \

        pulp_smash/__main__.py \
        pulp_smash/api.py \
        pulp_smash/cli.py \
        pulp_smash/config.py \
        pulp_smash/constants.py \
        pulp_smash/exceptions.py \
        pulp_smash/selectors.py \
        pulp_smash/utils.py
No config file found, using default configuration
pylint -j 4 --reports=n --disable=I,duplicate-code pulp_smash/tests/
No config file found, using default configuration
python -m unittest discover --start-directory tests --top-level-directory .
.........................................................
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 57 tests in 0.115s

OK
E
======================================================================
ERROR: tests (unittest2.loader._FailedTest)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
ImportError: Failed to import test module: tests
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash/lib/python3.4/site-packages/unittest2/loader.py", line 490, in _find_test_path
    package = self._get_module_from_name(name)
  File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash/lib/python3.4/site-packages/unittest2/loader.py", line 395, in _get_module_from_name
    __import__(name)
  File "/home/vagrant/devel/pulp-smash/pulp_smash/tests/__init__.py", line 41, in <module>
    import requests
  File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash/lib/python3.4/site-packages/requests/__init__.py", line 58, in <module>
    from . import utils
  File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash/lib/python3.4/site-packages/requests/utils.py", line 17, in <module>
    import platform
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.4/platform.py", line 115, in <module>
    import sys, os, re, subprocess
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 424, in <module>
    _PopenSelector = selectors.SelectSelector
AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'SelectSelector'


----------------------------------------------------------------------

FAILED (errors=1)
~

This is because Python 3 has a module called called selectors in the standard library, and pulp_smash.selectors is getting imported instead. This may happen due to the test runner altering the PYTHONPATH, but I have not confirmed that hypothesis.

Thanks to @seandst for assisting in finding the problem!

issue_title: Is MPI being used in baselines DDPG?
issue_body:
I don't see any reference to mpiexec when searching in the repo.  It it intended that we run with mpiexec to get a parallel version of DDPG?

eg I've tried this:
`mpiexec -n 4 python -m baselines.ddpg.main --env-id RoboschoolWalker2d-v1 --render`
(I don't have access to mujoco :( )

This runs 4 processes, but I'm not sure it's using episodes / transitions from the rank>0 processes in the rank 0 training...  At least I can't see how it would in the code, and the transition / frame rate seems the same as for a single one.  The only references I can see to MPI are functions to calculate mean, variance etc.  

This begs the question - is MPI really necessary or of any benefit here?  Is it used in some of the other agents, ie apart from DDPG?  Might you be able to refer me to another agent that does make real use of MPI?

Perhaps it could be removed if it's not being used; though I would equally appreciate any advocacy for MPI if it's doing something worthwhile.

Many thanks!

I have fixed it in a rather obscure way because I have not studied the library code well enough:

```python
        _meta = attrs.pop('_meta', None)
        if _meta:
            options = _meta
            added_options = {
                'name': name,
                'description': trim_docstring(attrs.get('__doc__')),
                'interfaces': (),
                'default_resolver': None,
                'local_fields': OrderedDict()
            }
            if attrs.get('Meta'):
                added_options.update(attrs.pop('Meta'))
            for key, val in added_options.items():
                if not hasattr(options, key):
                    setattr(options, key, val)
        else:
            options = Options(
                attrs.pop('Meta', None),
                name=name,
                description=trim_docstring(attrs.get('__doc__')),
                interfaces=(),
                default_resolver=None,
                local_fields=OrderedDict(),
            )
```

If this is good enough for now I can submit a PR.

issue_title: Certification test 9.2.8 fails for SED
issue_body:
When Active Timestamp or Pending Timestamp changes a parent should send unicast Data Response a SED. The Data Response message should contain Network Data (if changed), Active Timestamp and (optionally) Pending Timestamp. If SED detects that it has outdated Timestamps it should send Data Request to the parent. Parent responses with Data Response containing Active Dataset and Pending Dataset.

In current implementation when parent detects Timestamp change it sends Data Response containing Active Dataset and Pending Dataset to child (without waiting for Data Request). The test fails because Data Request is missing. 

There is one more problem: a parent sends all Datasets to SEDs whenever Network Data version changes. It causes unnecessary traffic to SEDs.

issue_title: joiner does not reset Crc16 calculations after joining attempt
issue_body:
When the commissioner calculates the steeringdata, `Crc16::mCrc` is always `0` at the start of each calculation.
When the joiner calculates the steeringdata, `Crc16::mCrc` is only `0` at boot or after a reset, but not at each join attempt. Therefore if a joiner tries to join a second time (the first attempt might have failed), the steeringdata are calculated incorrectly and the joining fails.

`src/core/meshcop/joiner.cpp` on the lines `107-111` should start from `Crc16::mCrc=0` instead of from a previously calculated version

issue_title: settings: handling compatibility between different versions
issue_body:
As raised by @nibanks in #1423, we need to establish some method for handling different versions of settings stored using the `otPlatSettings*` API.  This issue is solicit thoughts around what method to use.

As a starting point, it seems like backward compatibility is required to allow migration from an older version of OpenThread to a newer version.  However, is forward compatibility also required - to allow migration from a newer version of OpenThread back to an older version?

If both forward and backward compatibility is required, I'd argue that in most cases, settings structures are only allowed to add fields by appending to the end.  Fields should generally not be removed.

issue_title: Time series forecasting gap between prediction and reality
issue_body:
First thanks you for your attention on this post.

I'm using Tflearn for time series forecasting (regression problem). After lot of trouble on some part my network finally work and the training go well. I just have a question:
- Like seen in this excel file [PredictionandReality.zip](https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/files/1206680/prediction1Training0Epoch100.zip) I have a lag problem, my prediction value start increasing/decreasing BEFORE my real value (I thought it would be the other sense).
What could be the problem here?

Here is my training code:
```
import numpy as np
import tflearn
from pandas import DataFrame
from pandas import Series
from pandas import concat
from pandas import read_csv
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
import datetime
import tensorflow as tf
import os

def preprocess(data):
    return np.array(data, dtype=np.int32)


def parser(x):
    return datetime.datetime.strptime(x, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')


# frame a sequence as a supervised learning problem
def timeseries_to_supervised(data, lag=1):
    df = DataFrame(data)
    columns = [df.shift(i) for i in range(1, lag + 1)]
    columns.append(df)
    df = concat(columns, axis=1)
    df.fillna(0, inplace=True)
    return df


def difference(dataset, interval=1):
    diff = list()
    for i in range(interval, len(dataset)):
        value = dataset[i] - dataset[i - interval]
        diff.append(value)
    return Series(diff)


# scale train and test data to [-1, 1]
def scale(train):
    # fit scaler
    scaler = MinMaxScaler(feature_range=(-1, 1))
    scaler = scaler.fit(train)
    # transform train
    train = train.reshape(train.shape[0], train.shape[1])
    train_scaled = scaler.transform(train)
    return scaler, train_scaled


def fit_lstm(train, batch_size, nb_epoch, x, timesteps, training):
    X, y = train[0:-1], train[:, -1]
    X = X[:, 0].reshape(len(X), timesteps, 1)
    y = y.reshape(len(y), 1)

    # init
    tflearn.init_graph(num_cores=8, gpu_memory_fraction=0.5)

    # Build neural network

    net = tflearn.input_data(shape=[None, 1, 1])
    tnorm = tflearn.initializations.uniform(minval=-1.0, maxval=1.0)
    net = tflearn.lstm(net, timesteps, dropout=0.8)

    net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 1, activation='linear', weights_init=tnorm)
    net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.01,
                             loss='mean_square', metric='R2')


    # Define model
    model = tflearn.DNN(net, clip_gradients=0., tensorboard_verbose=3, tensorboard_dir="logs",)

    model.fit(X, y, n_epoch=nb_epoch, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False, show_metric=True, validation_set=0.1)
    model.save(os.path.join('/directory/path/','model.tflearn'))

def training(spotId):
    training = [0]
    epoch = [25]
    for x in training:
        for y in epoch:
            tf.reset_default_graph()
            # Load CSV file, indicate that the first column represents labels
            data = read_csv('nowcastScaled'+str(spotId)+'.csv', header=0, parse_dates=[0], index_col=0, squeeze=True, date_parser=parser)

            # transform data to be stationary
            raw_values = data.values
            diff_values = difference(raw_values, 1)

            # transform data to be supervised learning
            supervised = timeseries_to_supervised(diff_values, 1)
            supervised_values = supervised.values

            # split data into train and test-sets
            train = supervised_values[x:]

            # transform the scale of the data
            scaler, train_scaled = scale(train)
            # fit the model
            fit_lstm(train_scaled, 32, y, spotId, 1, x)
```
and here is my prediction code:
```
import numpy
import numpy as np
import tflearn
from pandas import DataFrame
from pandas import Series
from pandas import concat
from pandas import read_csv
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
import csv
import datetime
import os
import tensorflow as tf
# Preprocessing function
from tflearn import Accuracy, Momentum, dropout


def preprocess(data):
    return np.array(data, dtype=np.int32)


def parser(x):
    return datetime.datetime.strptime(x, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')


# frame a sequence as a supervised learning problem
def timeseries_to_supervised(data, lag=1):
    df = DataFrame(data)
    columns = [df.shift(i) for i in range(1, lag + 1)]
    columns.append(df)
    df = concat(columns, axis=1)
    df.fillna(0, inplace=True)
    return df


def difference(dataset, interval=1):
    diff = list()
    for i in range(interval, len(dataset)):
        value = dataset[i] - dataset[i - interval]
        diff.append(value)
    return Series(diff)


# invert differenced value
def inverse_difference(history, yhat, interval=1):
    return yhat + history[-interval]


# scale train and test data to [-1, 1]
def scale(train):
    # fit scaler
    scaler = MinMaxScaler(feature_range=(-1, 1))
    scaler = scaler.fit(train)
    # transform train
    train = train.reshape(train.shape[0], train.shape[1])
    train_scaled = scaler.transform(train)
    return scaler, train_scaled


# inverse scaling for a forecasted value
def invert_scale(scaler, X, value):
    T = list()
    T.append(X)
    new_row = [x for x in T] + [value]
    array = numpy.array(new_row)
    array = array.reshape(-1, len(array))
    inverted = scaler.inverse_transform(array)
    return inverted[0, -1]


# make a one-step forecast
def forecast_lstm2(model, X):
    X = X.reshape(-1, 1, 1)
    yhat = model.predict(X)
    return yhat[0, 0]


def prediction(spotId):
    training = [0]
    epoch = [25]
    for x in training:
        for e in epoch:
            tf.reset_default_graph()

            # Load CSV file, indicate that the first column represents labels
            data = read_csv('nowcastScaled'+str(spotId)+'.csv', header=0, parse_dates=[0], index_col=0, squeeze=True, date_parser=parser)

            # transform data to be stationary
            raw_values = data.values
            diff_values = difference(raw_values, 1)

            # transform data to be supervised learning
            supervised = timeseries_to_supervised(diff_values, 1)
            supervised_values = supervised.values

            # split data into train and test-sets
            train = supervised_values[x:]

            # transform the scale of the data
            scaler, train_scaled = scale(train)
            # Build neural network
            net = tflearn.input_data(shape=[None, 1, 1])
            tnorm = tflearn.initializations.uniform(minval=-1.0, maxval=1.0)
            net = tflearn.lstm(net, 1, dropout=0.8)
            net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 1, activation='linear', weights_init=tnorm)
            net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.01,
                                     loss='mean_square', metric='R2')
            lstm_model = tflearn.DNN(net, clip_gradients=0.)
            lstm_model.load(os.path.join('/directory/path/','model.tflearn'))

            # forecast the entire training dataset to build up state for forecasting
            train_reshaped = train_scaled[:, 0].reshape(len(train_scaled), 1, 1)
            lstm_model.predict(train_reshaped)
            # walk-forward validation on the test data
            predictions = list()
            predictionFeeder = list()
            X, y = train_scaled[0, 0:-1], train_scaled[0, -1]
            predictionFeeder.append(X)
            for i in range(0, 10000):
                # make one-step forecast
                yhat = forecast_lstm2(lstm_model, predictionFeeder[i])
                predictionFeeder.append(yhat)
                # invert scaling
                yhat2 = invert_scale(scaler, predictionFeeder[i + 1], yhat)
                yhat3 = inverse_difference(raw_values, yhat2, 10000 + 1 - i)
                predictions.append(yhat3)
```
Do you know what could be the reason for this problem?
For information I use 2 excel file, one with only training data (400 000 of training data with the timestamp one it) and 1 who have the value I'm trying to predict (10 000 of data, only using it after prediction to put both prediction and reality on an excel file). 
I separated them to be sure the testing set would never be used for training/predicting (when the data are on the same file suddenly my prediction are accurate and no time gap).
Thank you for your time and your help!

issue_title: Fix fabfile.py to upload using twine
issue_body:
[PEP566](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0566/#description-content-type-optional) and pypi.org now finally natively support markdown in the long description, see https://github.com/pypa/warehouse/issues/869. To take advantage of this feature you have to upload with `twine`.

In addition, https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/distributing-packages/#uploading-your-project-to-pypi seems to indicate that `python setup.py upload` may not always be secure, and they suggest using `twine` instead.

issue_title: pyscript command should pass an expanded absolute path to the py('run()') command
issue_body:
The new **pyscript** command should pass expand the script path before passing it to the **py run()** command.

This will enable using **~** user path expansion in the path to a Python script.

issue_title: StubbornDict class appears to be unecessary
issue_body:
It looks like StubbornDict is used only to construct and setup the self.settable custom dictionary.

It seems like a collections.OrderedDict can handle this purpose without needed to define a custom class.  This would cleanup and simplify the codebase a tiny bit.

issue_title: Unhandled exception in history command introduced by recent changes
issue_body:
A bug was introduced by the recent changes which merged the list functionality into the history command.

When attempting to do a span of history indices, an index error can occur and that exception needs to be caught and default history behavior needs to occur at that point instead.

issue_title: conda install version 0.3.0
issue_body:
I used 

`conda install -c glemaitre imbalanced-learn `

to install Imbalanced-learn. Instead of getting version 0.3.0, I have the older version

```
#
imbalanced-learn          0.2.1                    py27_0    glemaitre
```


How do I  install version 0.3.0 via conda install?

issue_title: Raise error if no minority/majority class
issue_body: There is no exception raise if the classes are already balanced. We should have a check in the base class.

issue_title: Sparse matrics
Add stop/start methods for containers.
issue_body:
Add methods in raml
And implement in grid services.

Actions already exist in the ays service

issue_title: Webhooks in mailgun backend only work with newest 2.7 pythons
issue_body:
`hmac.compare_digest` is [new in 2.7.7](https://docs.python.org/2/library/hmac.html#hmac.compare_digest). Debian Wheezy, LTS supported until May 2018, is [currently at 2.7.3](https://packages.debian.org/search?keywords=python2.7&searchon=names&exact=1&suite=all&section=all).

As a consequence, trying to use Mailgun mailhooks on Wheezy fails.

I suppose the fix is [something like this](https://github.com/liris/websocket-client/commit/4d5bc1e4e3622265ef7dc54281c0f73bb4dd98d6), along perhaps with a deprecation notice or similar?

issue_title: failure to match std::complex<double> to python complex
Use-after-free in object::memoryview for the format description
issue_body:
## Issue description

`object:memoryview` pass a temporary buffer for the format to `PyMemoryView_FromBuffer`. By reading the source code of this function, it does not "`strdup`" this format string, so it supposes it's always valid.

It is not really clear in the documentation how ownership works for the format. I figured this out because I had broken format issues with memory views in https://github.com/aguinet/dragonffi/ . A temporary fix by using `strdup` here https://github.com/aguinet/dragonffi/blob/master/third-party/pybind11/pytypes.h#L1255 fix the problem, but leaks memory.

## Reproducible example code

Hard to reproduce, as it's an use-after-free problem. Basically, creates a memoryview with a given format, and in some cases the format within the Python object will be wrong!
