Hello Folks,

I Can't connect to a Active Directory server using the ldapdb module. Everytime I try, the following error occurs:

'info': '000004DC: LdapErr: DSID-0C0906E8, comment: In order to perform this operation a successful bind must be completed on the connection., data 0, v1db1', 'desc': 'Operations error'}.

After this, I tried to turn the TLS on through the CONNECTION_OPTIONS:

    'USER': 'mydn',
    'PASSWORD': 'mypasswd',
    'CONNECTION_OPTIONS': {

    }
```

But The same error ocurred.

Currently, all my django apps that relies on ldap to perform something, uses the following options for ldap (global, not connection specfic):

```
    ldap.set_option(ldap.OPT_REFERRALS, 0)
    ldap.set_option(ldap.OPT_X_TLS_REQUIRE_CERT, 0)

With this, I can access the Active Directory Normally.

If I put these options immediately after the ensure method(line 98, base.py module), all works fine and all queries works very well.

So, I ask: How to pass arguments or get active directory integration to work on ldapdb?

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Check unchecked types in certbot-dns-dnsimple
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This issue is for checking untyped definitions in the `certbot-dns-dnsimple` module.

To start, add the following to `mypy.ini` in the root of the repo:
```
[mypy-certbot_dns_dnsimple.*]
check_untyped_defs = True
```

Then run `tox -e mypy`. If passes, submit a PR. If not, fix errors and submit a PR when tests pass. You can read more about how all of this works below.

See explanation here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fp0tPP9bzYkUViTnOXAyoT_KfgTfs8HSpNMoU8Tm_SY/edit?usp=sharing

From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the `--check-untyped-defs` option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation! 

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean `--check-untyped-defs` output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase. 

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!). 

Once `mypy --check-untyped-defs` passes on your codebase, you should lock down your progress by running the mypy checker in your CI environment.

Because mypy type annotations are optional, once you’ve done the setup above, you can start annotating your codebase at whatever pace you like. Over time, you’ll get the benefits of static types in the parts of the codebase that you’ve annotated, without needing to do anything special to the rest of the codebase (the wonders of gradual typing!). In the next section, I’ll discuss strategy for how to move the codebase towards being fully annotated.

issue_title: Make modification_check.sh more flexible
issue_body: Currently Travis tests will fail if `{certbot,letsencrypt}-auto` in the root of our repo isn't the same as the version from the most recent release tag. The result of this is Travis tests will fail from the time the release is pushed live until the PR incorporating `certbot-auto` changes is merged. Instead, we should allow `certbot-auto` to either be the version from the most recent release tag or the version currently in `master`.

issue_title: Incorrect wildcard redirects with Apache
issue_body:
The following vhost:
```
<VirtualHost *:80>

	ServerName example.com
	ServerAlias *.example.com
...
```
turns into this after requesting a redirect for `example.com`:
```
<VirtualHost *:80>
	ServerName example.com
	ServerAlias *.example.com
...
RewriteEngine on
RewriteCond %{SERVER_NAME} =*.example.com [OR]
RewriteCond %{SERVER_NAME} =example.com
RewriteRule ^ https://%{SERVER_NAME}%{REQUEST_URI} [END,QSA,R=permanent]
</VirtualHost>
```

`RewriteCond %{SERVER_NAME} =*.example.com` doesn't have the desired behavior. Here, `{%SERVER_NAME}` needs to be the exact string `*.example.com` or it doesn't match.

issue_title: Make the number of images to hold configurable.
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
There might be a case where a top-most consumer drops images due to its performance; assuming a case where the consumer tries to get images but some of them have been dropped. To prevent such a situation Harvester Core should be able to hold an arbitrary number of images on behalf of the consumer until it turns available.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Make the number of images to hold configurable.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
No.

**Additional context**
No.

issue_title: Outputs '# SKIP' on a test that is actually run
issue_body:
In pytest, a 'strict' xfail test is one that is expected to fail, and when it passes, that is considered to be a test failure.
I have the following test
```
import pytest
@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True)
def test_fail():
    # This errors, which means the test passes
    assert 1 == 2
```
which reports:
```
ok 1 - test_fail # SKIP 
1..1
```
The 'ok' part is correct, but the 'SKIP' is incorrect, since it did in fact run the test. (This can confuse the user, especially when obfuscated by a TAP driver)

Interestingly, the test 
```
import pytest
@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True)
def test_fail():
    # This test case doesn't error, which means the test should fail
    assert 1 == 1
```
reports:
```
not ok 1 - test_fail
# [XPASS(strict)]
1..1
```
Which is correct and doesn't have the above problem. However, when 'strict=False', it still fails, which isn't correct (the strict flag seems to be ignored in all cases).

issue_title: Use `require` and `assert` in the smart contracts
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Since version 0.4.10 solidity has introduced the `assert()` and `require()` [commands](https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/develop/control-structures.html?highlight=assert). Our smart contracts don't use them yet and it would be beneficial to start using them for both readability but also for assisting with future formal verification tools.

## Solution

As stated in the [documentation](https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/develop/control-structures.html?highlight=assert):

>If contracts are written so that `assert` is only used to test internal conditions and `require` is used in case of malformed input, a formal analysis tool that verifies that the invalid opcode can never be reached can be used to check for the absence of errors assuming valid inputs.

we should try to replace all `if (bad_condition) { throw; }` statements with `assert` and `revert` where appropriate.

## Tasklist

- [ ] All checks that have to do with invalid user input (such as [this](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a62adaaf374f0188990dfe324bcc9f73ef75cd37/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L221)) or invalid user action (such as [this](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a62adaaf374f0188990dfe324bcc9f73ef75cd37/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L133)) should be replaced with `require`
- [ ] If there is any check that is there to protect against programming error and generally things that should never happen but **do not** depend on user input then it should be replaced with an `assert`
- [ ] Replace the solidity pragma with `pragma solidity ^0.4.10` to indicate we now need newer solidity to use the `require` and `assert`;

issue_title: AttributeError: 'ChannelSerialization' object has no attribute 'block_number'
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

When restarting a Raiden node after having done some transactions that goes to the blockchain, there is a problem with recovering. More specifically with the ChannelSerialization object not having a `block_number` attribute:
```
AttributeError: 'ChannelSerialization' object has no attribute 'block_number'
```
https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/9650f9ce94b075f4ae12c7bfffdfaca582bbcec7/raiden/channel/netting_channel.py#L817

Full stack: https://gist.github.com/agatsoh/26f4329193eeac84d2d1f33012d6d2d9

## Solution

~Add a `block_number` attribute to the `ChannelSerialization` object~
The error is most likely due to this line: https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/9650f9ce94b075f4ae12c7bfffdfaca582bbcec7/raiden/raiden_service.py#L564
It seems that `block_number` is unused here https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/1b59f65939a01cbac1f187870b6c802a2006765a/raiden/network/channelgraph.py#L222
Removing the `block_number` arguments seems to be the solution


## Tasklist

~- [ ] add `block_number` attribute to `ChannelSerialization` object.~
- [ ] remove `block_number` argument from `add_channel` https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/1b59f65939a01cbac1f187870b6c802a2006765a/raiden/network/channelgraph.py#L222

issue_title: Unreliable health check
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Multiple users are reporting problems with the health check (#1020 #1086 also on gitter).

The problem is that even with the nodes online the health check is not changing the state of the nodes to `reachable` and the transfer fails because there is no route available. Some of these users are running all nodes and opening direct channels, so it's known that both nodes are online and there is a route.

This needs further investigation, the expected behavior is:

- On a fresh run (without a database):
  - The node will query the blockchain and get a [list of all channel that it is a participant](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/raiden_service.py#L728-L741).
  - For each of its partners a health check will be [started](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/raiden_service.py#L297-L304)
- For a running node:
  - The node queries the [blockchain each block for events](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/raiden_service.py#L278)
  - When a [new channel is found](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/event_handler.py#L207-L208), it's [registered and the healthcheck is started](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/event_handler.py#L244-L250)

From the workflows outlined above, participants of the same channel are pinging each other using the public ip / port, that should be sufficient for nat travesal allowing the nodes to communicate.

issue_title: Abandoning compatibility with old bdo2txt
issue_body:
Questions:
 - default converter writes number in Fortran formatting (0.100D+06, 0.0000000E+00), while python is using different formatting. Fluka is also using Fortran formatting. Should it be kept  ?
Pros: easier comparison of text output with Fluka (making plain text diff)
Cons: for users it probably doesn't matter, fortran formatting is time consuming when converting large files, extra code can be removed from repo.

- default converter keeps header in same way as bdo2txt. Removing compatibility allows us to redesign header field.

- we have now two converters producing ASCII files: default and plotdata. They can be merge into one and decision on the number of columns moved to a switch. Lets take 1-D scoring (i.e. dose profile) which gives us 1 variable axis (column) and 1 column of data. Two columns are fixed. We could have following behaviour:
  - `convertmc dose.bdo` will produce plain ASCII file with 5 columns (X,Y,Z, data, errors)
  - `convertmc dose.bdo --plotdata` will produce file with 3 columns(Z,data, errors) automatically figuring out which column is variable. X and Y position can be saved to header.
for gnuplot it would work this way:
  - `convertmc dose.bdo --converter default, gnuplot` will file with 5 columns and gnuplot script reading from a such file.
  - `convertmc dose.bdo --converter default, gnuplot --plotdata` will produce file with 3 columns and gnuplot script reading from 3-columns file.

issue_title: Bug when multiple wait requests involve the same object ID.
issue_body:
The following hangs (one of the calls to `ray.wait` inside of `g`  hangs).

```python
import ray
import time

ray.init(num_workers=3)

@ray.remote
def g(l):
  ray.wait([l[0]])

@ray.remote
def f(x):
  time.sleep(x)

x = f.remote(5)

ray.get([g.remote([x]), g.remote([x])])
```

The issue is that in `plasma_manager.c`, when we process a new object ID and call `update_object_wait_requests`, we iterate over a list (the utarray `object_wait_reqs->wait_requests`) of wait requests involving that object ID. For some of the wait requests, we return to the client by calling `return_from_wait`, which removes an element from `object_wait_reqs->wait_requests`. Since we delete an element from the array while iterating over it, we end up not iterating over the full list and not returning for all of the wait requests.

issue_title: StaticField is not compatible with widget ReadOnlyPasswordHashWidget
issue_body:
I am trying to replace the django admin template and using django-crispy forms for rendering the forms, but I had a problem with StaticField when rendering ReadOnlyPasswordHashWidget. The hashed value is rendered instead, which is not I wanted. 

Another thing I tried to do was adding a css_class "form-control-static" directly to the Field(), but it won't work, since crispy_field tag ensures that the class form-control will be added in the final.

Some possible solution I thought to deal with this:
- Create a css class to 'readonlypasswordhashwidget', which is not a good idea
- Create a new template for this case, which also is not a good idea
- Create the possibility to choose whether widget should be ignored or not. issue_title: ParallelSSHClient: timeout option with proxy host
issue_body:
Hi,

I have just tried to test with a Python script ParallelSSHClient and run_command with approximately 50 hosts behind a proxy host. 

I've realized that if you had one host in your host list which didn't boot correctly but you still establish a blocking TCP connexion the run_command return the output after bit more two minutes (eg. raise exception Connect Failed). So I decided to pass timeout option to the client with 10 seconds but the behaviour is the same and I must wait two minutes. So I pushed my script on the proxy host and launch ParallelSSHClient without proxy_host option and the output is returned after 10 seconds with an exception as expected (eg. timed out) . 

Can you confirm me this behaviour and how I should apply a timeout for my hosts list SSH connection when I specify a proxy_host ? Indeed I don't want to wait two minutes in the case of all hosts boot correctly except one.

You can see below my script and the result of the tests with one host.

Thanks in advance for your help

NOTE1 : If I want a timeout of 10 seconds I must specify .10 (with 10 I must wait 40 seconds).
NOTE2 : I notice in your [documentation](http://parallel-ssh.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pssh_client.html) that you have a timeout=120 in ParallelSSHClient method and a typo in docstring with "Defaults to 10".

script
-------------
```
#!/usr/bin/env python

from __future__ import print_function
import sys
import time
from pssh import ParallelSSHClient

def main(args):
    start_time = time.time()
    if len(args) > 1:
        client = ParallelSSHClient([args[0]],
                                   user='root',
                                   proxy_host=args[1],
                                   proxy_user=args[2],
                                   timeout=.10,
                                   channel_timeout=.10)
    else:
        client = ParallelSSHClient(args,
                                   user='root',
                                   timeout=.10,
                                   channel_timeout=.10)
    output = client.run_command("uptime", stop_on_errors=False)
    end_time = time.time() - start_time
    print('run_command duration : %d seconds' % end_time)
    print(output)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main(sys.argv[1:])

```

issue_title: ENH: Image should support rgb/rgba data
issue_body:
To be able to show 'raw' numpy arrays, we could support rgb and rgba format. I propose that the [format trait](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/ee16fb847e5b16e024fccc0fbd36a98d64918819/ipywidgets/widgets/widget_image.py#L43) support 'rgb' and 'rgba', which assumed the value contains rgb or rgba data in regular format (height, width, channel). 

From the frontend side, this requires converting the rgb(a) data to for instance png encoded data. This can be done with a non-attached canvas element. Also, if we add two methods to ImageModel:
 * setDataFrom(data, format)
 * getDataAs(format)
which would convert from to the supported formats to Image's own format type, we could support for instance the following use case:
 * create a ipywebrtc.CameraStream that has an image attached (API under discussion with @martinRenou)
 * take a snapshot (by sending an event), which can be grabbed as png encoded data at the frontend
 * the CameraStream widget will call imagemodel.setDataFrom(pngdata, 'png)
 * If the Image widget's format was set to 'rgb', the backend would be able to grab the image data and put it into a numpy array for analysis.

Feedback would be appriciated, I discussed it with @SylvainCorlay and he seemed to like it. If we get green light I may have found a new contributor.

issue_title: High level API for accessing getsockname() / getpeername()
issue_body:
There needs to be some way to get at the information in `getsockname` and `getpeername` from the high-level `Stream` and `Listener` interfaces.

It should involve an `await`, to support the PROXY protocol.

What should we do with `SSLStream`/`SSLListener`? There's a conceptual problem where the stream *they're* talking about doesn't exactly have an address, it's "connect to the transport's address AND THEN wrap the thing in a `SSLStream`". But then, it's not like `getsockname`/`getpeername` exactly has any meaning for streams anyway -- like, if you have a connected socket it's useful to be able to ask it what the two halves of the TCP 5-tuple looks like, but you can't actually *do* anything with this, it doesn't satisfy any invariants. Except "this might be useful for forensics".

So maybe pragmatics beats purity and `SSLStream` should just proxy this through to the underlying transport, and we'll leave the user to figure out how to decipher it.

Maybe a `SocketAddress` object that has `family` and `sockaddr` fields, plus possibly `host`, `port`, `path`, etc., as appropriate for the particular address?

issue_title: Use native traceback mutation on python 3.7
issue_body:
Since https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/4793 was merged, it looks like traceback objects will support Python-level instantiation and mutation in 3.7. We should use that instead of the current ctypes horrors.

We should also hassle the PyPy devs about implementing this. (In some ways this is even more interesting, because we're more aggressive about dropping support for old PyPy versions.)

Gazetteer example has a poor recall rate (under 28%)
issue_body:
I get a poor recall rate when running a Gazetteer example which I've created based on pgsql_big_dedupe_example.
The code for reproducing the problem is attached. Please see the "instructions.txt" file for instructions.

     https://github.com/dedupeio/dedupe/files/1134932/Gazetteer.Problem.zip 

The code actually uses the results of pgsql_big_dedupe_example. For every cluster returned by Dedupe.MatchBlocks (having more than one record), one donor is selected for the "gazetteer" while the other donors are used as "messy data". It means that for each "messy" donor, there should be a match in the gazetteer which was already  identified by Dedupe as the same donor (when pgsql_big_dedupe_example was run).
Under these circumstances, I would expect this Gazetteer example to have a very high recall (and also precision). But while the precision is indeed high, the recall is very poor.

The "instructions.txt" file also contains instructions for checking the results to calculate the recall ratio, 
 which is under 28%.

I use Dedupe version 1.7.0, with Python 3.5.3.

The recall argument for Gazetteer.train is 0.9 .
The threshold for Gazetteer.matchBlocks is 0.5 .

Notes:

	1. Because of issue #578 (which wasn't fixed yet in 1.7.0 - see issue #587), the blocks are created 
              using StaticDedupe.blocker rather than Gazetteer.blocker. 

	2. The file gazetteer_problem_settings is included in the set of files. But if deleted, the example code
	    would re-create it.

	3. The file dedupe_example_settings is also included, for creating the StaticDedupe instace.
	   If deleted, the example code would NOT run. However, it may be re-created by running 
             pgsql_big_dedupe_example using the same training JSON file: gazeteer_problem_training.json .

issue_title: change the way to give a progressReporter to a task
issue_body:
At the moment, a progressReporter is given to a task as an argument:
https://github.com/alphatwirl/alphatwirl/blob/dd0a7a38f06c52c73b58be976c4d2331f3e837ee/alphatwirl/concurrently/Worker.py#L24-L29
this potentially causes a name conflict if a task already has an argument `progressReporter` for a different purpose.

Instead of `Worker` giving the reporter to a task, the task should find the reporter if the task wants a reporter.

A possible solution: a worker register a reporter to a DB, e.g., `ProgressReporterAgency` or `ProgressReporterOffice` defined in the module `progressbar`. A task gets the reporter from the DB.

issue_title: 🚀🚀 Real Time Object Detection in TensorLayer
issue_body:
A discussion for real-time object detection using TensorLayer and TensorFlow

- For Model Acceleration, see https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/issues/416

### Paper List
* PVANET [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1608.08021) [[Code]](https://github.com/sanghoon/pva-faster-rcnn)
* OverFeat, NYU [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1312.6229.pdf)
* R-CNN, UC Berkeley [[Paper-CVPR14]](http://www.cv-foundation.org/openaccess/content_cvpr_2014/papers/Girshick_Rich_Feature_Hierarchies_2014_CVPR_paper.pdf) [[Paper-arXiv14]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1311.2524)
* SPP, Microsoft Research [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1406.4729) 
* Fast R-CNN, Microsoft Research [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1504.08083)
* Faster R-CNN, Microsoft Research [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1506.01497)
* R-CNN minus R, Oxford [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1506.06981)
* End-to-end people detection in crowded scenes [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/abs/1506.04878)
* Yolo1: Unified, Real-Time Object Detection [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/abs/1506.02640), [[Paper Version 2]](https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.08242), [[C Code]](https://github.com/pjreddie/darknet), [[Tensorflow Code]](https://github.com/thtrieu/darkflow)
* 🚀 Yolo2: [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.08242), [[C Code]](https://github.com/pjreddie/darknet)
* Inside-Outside Net [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.04143)
* Deep Residual Network (Current State-of-the-Art) [[Paper]](http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385)
* Weakly Supervised Object Localization with Multi-fold Multiple Instance Learning [[Paper](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1503.00949.pdf)]
* R-FCN [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/1605.06409) [[Code]](https://github.com/daijifeng001/R-FCN)
* 🚀 SSD [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1512.02325v2.pdf) [[Code]](https://github.com/weiliu89/caffe/tree/ssd)
* Speed/accuracy trade-offs for modern convolutional object detectors [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1611.10012v1.pdf)

### Datasets
Popular datasets for object detection.

* MSCOCO [[link]](http://cocodataset.org/#home)
* VOC2007/2012 [[2007]](http://host.robots.ox.ac.uk/pascal/VOC/voc2007/) [[2012]](http://host.robots.ox.ac.uk/pascal/VOC/voc2012/) : 20 classes, and 3 extra classes for person.
* MPII [[link]](http://human-pose.mpi-inf.mpg.de/#overview)
* ImageNet

### Existing code
- data augmentation using TensorLayer [tl.prepro.obj_box](http://tensorlayer.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modules/prepro.html#object-detection) ,  [知乎](https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/31466173)
- load VOC dataset using TensorLayer [tl.files.load_voc_dataset](http://tensorlayer.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modules/files.html#voc-2007-2012)
- [darkflow](https://github.com/thtrieu/darkflow) incorrect implementation for YOLO2, don't use!
- [YAD2K: Yet Another Darknet 2 Keras](https://github.com/allanzelener/YAD2K) correct implementation.

### WIP
- YOLO2 (ignore YOLO1)
- SSD
- API to load MSCOCO dataset

### Others

issue_title: Keras-RL adding extra dimension to RGB input
issue_body:
Hi,
I have a custom environment which generates the following inputs:
1) RGB image (144, 256, 3)
2) Measurement Vector 1 


I use the Keras-RL MultiInputProcessor to feed each of these inputs to neural networks. For the neural network that processes RGB image i have defined the input shape as same as RGB image (H, W, 3).

The problem is that when i pass the model to the fit method, i am getting the following trace:

_File "DQN-Train.py", line 167, in <module>
    dqn.fit(env, callbacks=callbacks, nb_steps=1750000, visualize=False, verbose=0, log_interval=100)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/rl/core.py", line 170, in fit
    action = self.forward(observation)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/rl/agents/dqn.py", line 236, in forward
    q_values = self.compute_q_values(state)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/rl/agents/dqn.py", line 74, in compute_q_values
    q_values = self.compute_batch_q_values([state]).flatten()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/rl/agents/dqn.py", line 67, in compute_batch_q_values
    q_values = self.model.predict_on_batch(batch)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/keras/engine/training.py", line 1279, in predict_on_batch
    x, _, _ = self._standardize_user_data(x)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/keras/engine/training.py", line 757, in _standardize_user_data
    exception_prefix='input')
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/keras/engine/training_utils.py", line 129, in standardize_input_data
    'with shape ' + str(data_shape))
ValueError: Error when checking input: expected input_1 to have 4 dimensions, but got array with shape (1, 1, 144, 256, 3)_

I checked the shape of the input at each stage
The env.RGB =(144,256,3)
observation.shape = (144,256,3)
The shape of the RGB image remain (144, 256,3) till call to this method : 

_batch = self.process_state_batch(state_batch)_ ( inside dqn.py file inside method compute_batch_q_values)
After this call, the shape of the RGB input changes from (144, 256, 3) to (1, 1, 144, 256, 3)

This leads to the error that we see in the stack trace. Is this a bug in Keras-RL or Am i missing something here?

Thanks
RL

issue_title: slow crc32
issue_body:
Please, use fast crc32 from zlib. It is about 18 times faster:

import zlib
import couchbase.crc32 as couch_slow_crc
import time

def Func1(amax=100000):
   t1=time.clock()
   for i in range(amax):
      x=couch_slow_crc.crc32_hash(`i`)
   t2=time.clock()
   return t2-t1
   
def Func2(amax=100000):
   t1=time.clock()
   for i in range(amax):
      x=(zlib.crc32(`i`)>>16)&0x7fff
   t2=time.clock()
   return t2-t1


def Func3(amax=100000):
   for i in range(amax):
      v1=(zlib.crc32(`i`)>>16)&0x7fff
      v2=couch_slow_crc.crc32_hash(`i`)
      if v1!=v2:
         print 'conflict %d %x %x'%(i,v1,v2)
   t2=time.clock()

t1=Func1()
t2=Func2()
print 'time couch',t1
print 'time zlib',t2
print 'speed',t1/t2
Func3()

#######################################
time couch 1.83402449707
time zlib 0.0990188503142
speed 18.5219732531

issue_title: Transactions fail with validation error for users with a positive balance
issue_body:
Here's a snippet that succeeds when the user has no account balance, but fails when they have an account balance and you submit a transaction for an amount smaller than their available balance:

    account = recurly.Account(account_code='jordan+oTAHmuzG@prehype.com')
    transaction = recurly.Transaction(amount_in_cents=1200, currency='USD', account=account)
    transaction.save()

In this example, I'm charging the user $12.  If their account has a current balance of $11 (or anything else less than $12), this call will succeed.  If their account has a balance of $13 (or anything greater than $12), this call will fail with a ValidationError.
